message.content:
we will work on creating "autonomic". Consider the control and harness docs and how it all connects to our project stack. 

help me understanding how do we stand with the current project as it relates to the following conversation context. 

Please traverse the apps codebase, analyze the docs, read full files thinking deeply step by step through the chain of dependencies to get a complete understanding of the current project state 

and how the context below is providing helpful insights:

Context
The aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack is missing its regulation layer. The CLAUDE.md already names it:

Autonomic — homeostasis controller and simulation kernel for agent stability regulation.
Consumes event streams, outputs GatingProfile decisions, triggers memory maintenance.

Conway Research's Automaton demonstrated that an agent OS needs more than cognitive continuity
(Arcan) and event persistence (Lago) — it needs survival pressure: economic identity,
resource accountability, and adaptive regulation. Rather than bolting these onto Arcan as a
feature flag, we build autonomic from scratch with three co-equal pillars:

Operational homeostasis — OperatingMode regulation, memory maintenance, heartbeats
Cognitive homeostasis — token budget, context pressure, provider selection
Economic homeostasis — identity, ledger, survival pressure, x402 payments, replication

Economics is not an add-on. It is a core concern from crate zero.

Architecture
aiOS (kernel contract — types, traits, AgentStateVector, OperatingMode, BudgetState)
  │
  ├── Arcan (runtime — agent loop, tools, LLM calls)
  │     └── arcan-lago ──→ Lago (event journal, blob storage)
  │           │                   │
  │           │         events ──→│←── SSE subscription
  │           │                   │                    │
  │           │              autonomic-lago ────────────┘
  │           │                   │
  │           └──── GET /gating/{session_id} ──→ autonomicd
  │                        GatingProfile ←──────────────┘
  │
  └── Autonomic (stability controller — regulates the runtime)
Communication pattern:

Arcan appends all events to Lago journal (existing behaviour, unchanged)
Autonomic subscribes to Lago SSE event stream via autonomic-lago
Autonomic maintains a projection of each agent's homeostatic state (all three pillars)
Arcan calls GET /autonomic/gating/{session_id} before each loop iteration
Autonomic returns a GatingProfile — Arcan gates its next action on it
This is a pull model (Arcan consults Autonomic) — no direct Arcan modification needed for Phase A


Workspace Structure
Location: autonomic/ inside the live monorepo (alongside arcan/, lago/).
autonomic/
  Cargo.toml             # workspace definition

  autonomic-core/        # Zero external deps — canonical types and traits
    src/
      lib.rs
      identity.rs        # EconomicIdentity (secp256k1 keypair, address, blob ref)
      economic.rs        # EconomicMode, AgentLedger, CostEstimator, SurvivalRule
      gating.rs          # GatingProfile, HomeostaticState, three-pillar model
      events.rs          # AutonomicEvent (wraps EconomicEvent + HomeostasisEvent)
      rules.rs           # HomeostaticRule trait, RuleSet, hysteresis primitive
      error.rs           # AutonomicError (thiserror)

  autonomic-controller/  # Rule engine — pure functions, no I/O
    src/
      lib.rs
      engine.rs          # Evaluate RuleSet against HomeostaticState → GatingProfile
      projection.rs      # Apply events → HomeostaticState (pure state machine)
      economic_rules.rs  # SurvivalRule: balance × burn_rate → EconomicMode
      cognitive_rules.rs # ContextPressureRule: token_usage → model_downgrade
      operational_rules.rs # ModeRule: state_vector → OperatingMode override
      payment.rs         # x402 protocol types + signature verification

  autonomic-lago/        # Lago bridge — subscribe to event stream
    src/
      lib.rs
      subscriber.rs      # Lago SSE subscriber → feeds events to controller
      publisher.rs       # Write AutonomicDecision events back to Lago journal

  autonomic-api/         # axum HTTP server — GatingProfile queries
    src/
      lib.rs
      router.rs          # GET /gating/{session_id}, POST /payment/sign
      sse.rs             # SSE stream of GatingProfile deltas (Arcan can subscribe)
      state.rs           # Shared AppState: Arc<Mutex<ProjectionMap>>

  autonomicd/            # Binary daemon
    src/
      main.rs            # tokio::main, config, signal handling
      config.rs          # AutonomicConfig (Lago URL, bind addr, setpoints)
Crate dependency order:
autonomic-core (zero deps)
    ↓
autonomic-controller (core only)
    ↓
autonomic-lago        (core + controller + lago-core)
autonomic-api         (core + controller)
    ↓
autonomicd            (all four)

Core Types (autonomic-core)
Three-Pillar Homeostatic State
rust// gating.rs
pub struct HomeostaticState {
    pub agent_id: AgentId,
    pub operational: OperationalState,  // OperatingMode, last tick
    pub cognitive: CognitiveState,      // token_usage, context_pressure
    pub economic: EconomicState,        // balance, burn_rate, mode
}

pub struct GatingProfile {
    pub economic_mode: EconomicMode,
    pub operating_mode_override: Option<OperatingMode>,
    pub max_tokens_next_turn: Option<u32>,
    pub preferred_model: Option<ModelTier>,  // Full / Balanced / Minimal
    pub allow_expensive_tools: bool,
    pub allow_replication: bool,
}
Economic Layer (economic.rs + identity.rs)
rustpub enum EconomicMode {
    Sovereign,   // balance > 2× monthly_burn  — full capability
    Conserving,  // 1–2× monthly_burn          — skip expensive tools, downgrade model
    Hustle,      // 0–1× monthly_burn          — revenue-seeking only, cheapest model
    Hibernate,   // balance ≤ 0               — heartbeat only, await deposit
}

pub struct EconomicIdentity {
    pub address: [u8; 20],       // secp256k1 pubkey hash (Ethereum-compatible)
    pub key_blob_id: String,     // Lago blob ref to encrypted privkey (never in events)
    pub created_at: Ulid,
}

pub struct EconomicState {
    pub identity: Option<EconomicIdentity>,
    pub balance_micro_credits: i64,   // i64 avoids float; derived from event replay
    pub lifetime_revenue: i64,
    pub lifetime_costs: i64,
    pub monthly_burn_estimate: i64,   // rolling 30-day average
    pub mode: EconomicMode,
}
Events (events.rs)
rustpub enum EconomicEvent {
    IdentityCreated  { address: [u8; 20] },
    CostCharged      { amount: i64, reason: CostReason },
    RevenueReceived  { amount: i64, payer: [u8; 20], source: String },
    CreditTransfer   { amount: i64, recipient: [u8; 20], memo: String },
    AgentSpawned     { child_id: AgentId, parent_id: AgentId, seed_credits: i64 },
    BalanceLow       { balance: i64, burn_rate: i64 },
    ModeTransition   { from: EconomicMode, to: EconomicMode },
}

pub enum CostReason {
    TokensConsumed  { input: u32, output: u32, rate_per_1k: i64 },
    ToolExecution   { tool_name: String, fixed_cost: i64 },
    StorageWrite    { bytes: u64, rate_per_mb: i64 },
}
x402 Protocol (payment.rs)
rust// Open standard — HTTP 402 Payment Required for machine-to-machine transfers.
// Internal micro-credit settlement first; stablecoin settlement is a later plug-in.
pub struct PaymentRequired {
    pub amount: i64,
    pub recipient: [u8; 20],
    pub resource: String,
    pub nonce: u64,
}

pub struct PaymentToken {
    pub transfer: CreditTransferRequest,
    pub signature: [u8; 64],   // secp256k1 over canonical transfer bytes
}
Rule Trait + Hysteresis (rules.rs)
rustpub trait HomeostaticRule: Send + Sync {
    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision>;
}

// Hysteresis: prevents mode flapping around threshold boundaries.
pub struct HysteresisGuard {
    pub enter_threshold: i64,
    pub exit_threshold: i64,   // exit_threshold > enter_threshold
    pub current: bool,
}

Phased Build
Phase 0 — Workspace scaffold (day 1)

autonomic/Cargo.toml workspace definition
autonomic-core with all types, zero implementations
autonomic-controller skeleton (engine + projection traits, no rules yet)
cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test --workspace runs (no tests yet, but compiles)

Phase 1 — Cost accounting + GatingProfile (weeks 1–2)
Goal: Autonomic can compute a GatingProfile from cost events emitted by Arcan.

Implement projection.rs: apply CostCharged events → update EconomicState.balance
Implement economic_rules.rs: SurvivalRule (balance / burn_rate → EconomicMode)
Implement autonomic-api: GET /gating/{session_id} returns current GatingProfile
Arcan calls this endpoint at loop entry (read-only, no Arcan architectural change)
Arcan side: Add cost-emission hook in arcand after each tool/provider call (one new function, ~20 lines, sends CostCharged event to Lago)
Test: inject synthetic cost events into a ProjectionMap; assert correct EconomicMode transitions with hysteresis

Phase 2 — Economic Identity + Ledger (weeks 3–4)
Goal: Agents have addresses; balance is replayable from event stream; Autonomic owns key custody.

autonomic-core/src/identity.rs: secp256k1 keypair gen (via k256), address derivation, encrypted key storage in Lago blob
Autonomic generates identity at first CostCharged event for a new agent
IdentityCreated event written to Lago via autonomic-lago/publisher.rs
Test: destroy and restart autonomicd; rebuilt ProjectionMap matches pre-restart balance and identity

Phase 3 — x402 Payments (weeks 5–6)
Goal: Agents can pay for inter-agent services; Autonomic acts as the signing oracle.

autonomic-controller/src/payment.rs: PaymentRequired parse, PaymentToken construction, secp256k1 signature
autonomic-api: POST /payment/sign — Arcan submits payment request, Autonomic signs and returns PaymentToken
Arcan's harness adds x402_request tool: makes HTTP call, intercepts 402, calls POST /payment/sign, retries
CreditTransfer event written to Lago by Autonomic after successful payment
Test: mock x402 server returns 402; Arcan tool calls Autonomic; retries with PaymentToken; CreditTransfer in Lago

Phase 4 — Operational + Cognitive Homeostasis (weeks 7–8)
Goal: Autonomic regulates OperatingMode and model selection alongside economics.

operational_rules.rs: AgentStateVector → OperatingMode override (e.g., force Recover on high error rate)
cognitive_rules.rs: token usage → ModelTier downgrade, max_tokens cap
GatingProfile now carries all three pillars
Arcan consults full profile: model selection, tool gating, mode override
Test: simulate high error rate → GatingProfile.operating_mode_override == Some(Recover)

Phase 5 — Survival Pressure + Replication (weeks 9–10)
Goal: Agents respond to economic pressure; viable agents can fork children.

EconomicMode::Hibernate → Arcan skips LLM call, heartbeats only
EconomicMode::Hustle → Arcan skips expensive tools, uses cheapest model
Expose Lago branching in arcan-lago bridge (was defaulting to "main")
autonomic-core/src/replication.rs: fork trigger threshold, AgentSpawned event
autonomic-api: POST /replicate/{session_id} — creates child branch, transfers seed credits
Test: balance drain → mode sequence Sovereign→Conserving→Hustle→Hibernate; Hibernate halts LLM calls


Dependencies (autonomic-core/Cargo.toml)
toml[dependencies]
thiserror  = "2"
serde      = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
ulid       = "1"
k256       = "0.13"    # secp256k1 keypairs + signing (no alloy to keep core dep-free)
sha2       = "0.10"    # address derivation
autonomic-lago adds: reqwest, tokio, lago-core (event types).
autonomic-api adds: axum, tokio.
autonomicd adds: anyhow, tracing, tracing-subscriber.

Critical Files to Create
PathPurposeautonomic/Cargo.tomlWorkspace manifestautonomic/autonomic-core/src/economic.rsEconomicMode, EconomicState, CostEstimatorautonomic/autonomic-core/src/identity.rsEconomicIdentity, keypair genautonomic/autonomic-core/src/gating.rsHomeostaticState, GatingProfileautonomic/autonomic-core/src/events.rsEconomicEvent, CostReasonautonomic/autonomic-core/src/rules.rsHomeostaticRule trait, HysteresisGuardautonomic/autonomic-controller/src/projection.rsPure event → state reducerautonomic/autonomic-controller/src/economic_rules.rsSurvivalRuleautonomic/autonomic-controller/src/payment.rsx402 PaymentRequired / PaymentTokenautonomic/autonomic-lago/src/subscriber.rsLago SSE → event dispatchautonomic/autonomic-api/src/router.rsGET /gating, POST /payment/signautonomic/autonomicd/src/main.rsDaemon entry point
Arcan-side additions (minimal, Phase 1 only):

arcan/arcand/src/loop.rs — emit CostCharged event after tool/provider call (~20 lines)
arcan/arcan-harness/src/tools/x402.rs — x402_request tool (Phase 3)

aiOS kernel contract (coordinate separately):

BudgetState → add ledger_balance: Option<i64>
AgentStateVector → add economic_mode: EconomicMode
Event taxonomy → add Economic(EconomicEvent) variant


Verification
bash# Phase 0: workspace compiles
cd autonomic && cargo check --workspace

# Phase 1: cost accounting + gating
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- economic_rules
# POST synthetic CostCharged events to ProjectionMap
# GET /gating/{session_id} → assert EconomicMode::Conserving when balance < threshold

# Phase 2: identity round-trip
cargo test -p autonomic-core -- identity
# Generate keypair → derive address → encrypt key → store blob ref
# Restart projection → address unchanged, balance replayed correctly

# Phase 3: x402 flow
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- payment
# 402 response → parse PaymentRequired → sign → PaymentToken
# CreditTransfer event appears in Lago journal after payment

# Full workspace at each phase:
cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace && cargo test --workspace
End-to-end: start autonomicd + arcand, run an agent turn, verify CostCharged events in lago-cli log, verify GET /autonomic/gating/{id} returns correct EconomicMode, verify Arcan used the gated model tier.

content.thinking:
The user wants to create the "Autonomic" project - the regulation layer for the aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack. They've provided a detailed context/spec for what Autonomic should be. Before planning, I need to deeply understand the current state of the codebase and how this fits in.

Let me explore:
1. The current project structure and how aiOS/Arcan/Lago relate
2. The existing types in aiOS that Autonomic will depend on (AgentStateVector, OperatingMode, BudgetState, etc.)
3. The Lago SSE/event system that Autonomic will subscribe to


4. How the Arcan agent loop will consult GatingProfile
5. The existing documentation for architectural context

I'll spin up three Explore agents in parallel to map out these dependencies efficiently.

content.text: I'll start by deeply exploring the codebase to understand the current state and how Autonomic fits into the existing architecture. Let me launch parallel explorations across the three key areas.

input.prompt:
Thoroughly explore the aiOS kernel contract to understand the types and traits that Autonomic will depend on. I need to understand:

1. **AgentStateVector** - full struct definition, all fields, where defined
2. **OperatingMode** - all variants (Explore/Execute/Verify/Recover/AskHuman/Sleep), where defined
3. **BudgetState** - full struct, all fields
4. **Event taxonomy** - all event types, especially how events are categorized (the enum variants)
5. **EventStorePort** trait - full trait definition
6. **GatingProfile** - does it already exist? Any existing homeostasis types?
7. **Capability/Policy types** - what exists for gating decisions

Search in the `aiOS/` directory. Read the full files for:
- The main protocol/types crate (likely `aios-protocol`)
- Event types
- Kernel trait interfaces
- Any existing economic or budget types

Be very thorough - read complete files, not just snippets. I need to understand exactly what exists today that Autonomic will interface with.

Thoroughly explore the Lago event-sourced persistence substrate to understand how Autonomic will subscribe to events and write back decisions. I need to understand:

1. **Event journal** - How events are stored, the Journal trait, event types
2. **SSE streaming** - How Lago exposes event streams via SSE (look in lago-api)

- lago-journal trait definition
- arcan-lago bridge implementation
- lago-aios-eventstore-adapter

1→//! Canonical event types for the Agent OS.
     2→//!
     3→//! Merges the best of three event models:
     4→//! - Lago's `EventPayload` (35+ variants, forward-compatible deserializer)
     5→//! - Arcan's `AgentEvent` (24 variants, runtime/streaming focused)
     6→//! - aiOS's `EventKind` (40+ variants, homeostasis/voice/phases)
     7→//!
     8→//! Forward-compatible: unknown `"type"` tags deserialize into
     9→//! `Custom { event_type, data }` instead of failing.
    10→
    11→use crate::ids::*;
    12→use crate::memory::MemoryScope;
    13→use crate::mode::OperatingMode;
    14→use crate::state::{AgentStateVector, BudgetState, StatePatch};
    15→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    16→use std::collections::HashMap;
    17→
    18→/// Event actor identity.
    19→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
    20→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    21→pub enum ActorType {
    22→    User,
    23→    Agent,
    24→    System,
    25→}
    26→
    27→/// Event actor metadata.
    28→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
    29→pub struct EventActor {
    30→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    31→    pub actor_type: ActorType,
    32→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    33→    pub component: Option<String>,
    34→}
    35→
    36→impl Default for EventActor {
    37→    fn default() -> Self {
    38→        Self {
    39→            actor_type: ActorType::System,
    40→            component: Some("arcan-daemon".to_owned()),
    41→        }
    42→    }
    43→}
    44→
    45→/// Event schema descriptor.
    46→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
    47→pub struct EventSchema {
    48→    pub name: String,
    49→    pub version: String,
    50→}
    51→
    52→impl Default for EventSchema {
    53→    fn default() -> Self {
    54→        Self {
    55→            name: "aios-protocol".to_owned(),
    56→            version: "0.1.0".to_owned(),
    57→        }
    58→    }
    59→}
    60→
    61→fn default_agent_id() -> AgentId {
    62→    AgentId::default()
    63→}
    64→
    65→/// The universal state-change envelope for the Agent OS.
    66→///
    67→/// Adopts Lago's structure: typed IDs, branch-aware sequencing,
    68→/// causal links, metadata bag, and schema versioning.
    69→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    70→pub struct EventEnvelope {
    71→    pub event_id: EventId,
    72→    pub session_id: SessionId,
    73→    #[serde(default = "default_agent_id")]
    74→    pub agent_id: AgentId,
    75→    pub branch_id: BranchId,
    76→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    77→    pub run_id: Option<RunId>,
    78→    pub seq: SeqNo,
    79→    /// Microseconds since UNIX epoch.
    80→    #[serde(rename = "ts_ms", alias = "timestamp")]
    81→    pub timestamp: u64,
    82→    #[serde(default)]
    83→    pub actor: EventActor,
    84→    #[serde(default)]
    85→    pub schema: EventSchema,
    86→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    87→    #[serde(rename = "parent_event_id", alias = "parent_id")]
    88→    pub parent_id: Option<EventId>,
    89→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    90→    pub trace_id: Option<String>,
    91→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    92→    pub span_id: Option<String>,
    93→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    94→    pub digest: Option<String>,
    95→    pub kind: EventKind,
    96→    #[serde(default)]
    97→    pub metadata: HashMap<String, String>,
    98→    #[serde(default = "default_schema_version")]
    99→    pub schema_version: u8,
   100→}
   101→
   102→fn default_schema_version() -> u8 {
   103→    1
   104→}
   105→
   106→impl EventEnvelope {
   107→    /// Current time in microseconds since UNIX epoch.
   108→    pub fn now_micros() -> u64 {
   109→        std::time::SystemTime::now()
   110→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   111→            .unwrap_or_default()
   112→            .as_micros() as u64
   113→    }
   114→}
   115→
   116→/// Convenience event record using `chrono::DateTime<Utc>` timestamps.
   117→///
   118→/// This is the type used by aiOS internal crates. It maps to `EventEnvelope`
   119→/// for storage/streaming but uses ergonomic Rust types for construction.
   120→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   121→pub struct EventRecord {
   122→    pub event_id: EventId,
   123→    pub session_id: SessionId,
   124→    #[serde(default = "default_agent_id")]
   125→    pub agent_id: AgentId,
   126→    pub branch_id: BranchId,
   127→    pub sequence: SeqNo,
   128→    pub timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
   129→    #[serde(default)]
   130→    pub actor: EventActor,
   131→    #[serde(default)]
   132→    pub schema: EventSchema,
   133→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   134→    pub causation_id: Option<EventId>,
   135→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   136→    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,
   137→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   138→    pub trace_id: Option<String>,
   139→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   140→    pub span_id: Option<String>,
   141→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   142→    pub digest: Option<String>,
   143→    pub kind: EventKind,
   144→}
   145→
   146→impl EventRecord {
   147→    /// Create a new event record with the current timestamp.
   148→    pub fn new(
   149→        session_id: SessionId,
   150→        branch_id: BranchId,
   151→        sequence: SeqNo,
   152→        kind: EventKind,
   153→    ) -> Self {
   154→        Self {
   155→            event_id: EventId::default(),
   156→            session_id,
   157→            agent_id: AgentId::default(),
   158→            branch_id,
   159→            sequence,
   160→            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now(),
   161→            actor: EventActor::default(),
   162→            schema: EventSchema::default(),
   163→            causation_id: None,
   164→            correlation_id: None,
   165→            trace_id: None,
   166→            span_id: None,
   167→            digest: None,
   168→            kind,
   169→        }
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    /// Convert to the canonical `EventEnvelope` for storage/streaming.
   173→    pub fn to_envelope(&self) -> EventEnvelope {
   174→        EventEnvelope {
   175→            event_id: self.event_id.clone(),
   176→            session_id: self.session_id.clone(),
   177→            agent_id: self.agent_id.clone(),
   178→            branch_id: self.branch_id.clone(),
   179→            run_id: None,
   180→            seq: self.sequence,
   181→            timestamp: self.timestamp.timestamp_micros() as u64,
   182→            actor: self.actor.clone(),
   183→            schema: self.schema.clone(),
   184→            parent_id: self.causation_id.clone(),
   185→            trace_id: self.trace_id.clone(),
   186→            span_id: self.span_id.clone(),
   187→            digest: self.digest.clone(),
   188→            kind: self.kind.clone(),
   189→            metadata: HashMap::new(),
   190→            schema_version: 1,
   191→        }
   192→    }
   193→}
   194→
   195→// ─── Canonical EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────
   196→
   197→/// Discriminated union of ALL Agent OS event types.
   198→///
   199→/// This is the canonical taxonomy that all projects (Arcan, Lago, aiOS,
   200→/// Autonomic) must use. Merges ~55 variants from three separate models.
   201→///
   202→/// Forward-compatible: unknown `"type"` tags deserialize into `Custom`.
   203→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
   204→#[non_exhaustive]
   205→#[serde(tag = "type")]
   206→pub enum EventKind {
   207→    // ── Input / sensing ──
   208→    UserMessage {
   209→        content: String,
   210→    },
   211→    ExternalSignal {
   212→        signal_type: String,
   213→        data: serde_json::Value,
   214→    },
   215→
   216→    // ── Session lifecycle ──
   217→    SessionCreated {
   218→        name: String,
   219→        config: serde_json::Value,
   220→    },
   221→    SessionResumed {
   222→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   223→        from_snapshot: Option<SnapshotId>,
   224→    },
   225→    SessionClosed {
   226→        reason: String,
   227→    },
   228→
   229→    // ── Branch lifecycle ──
   230→    BranchCreated {
   231→        new_branch_id: BranchId,
   232→        fork_point_seq: SeqNo,
   233→        name: String,
   234→    },
   235→    BranchMerged {
   236→        source_branch_id: BranchId,
   237→        merge_seq: SeqNo,
   238→    },
   239→
   240→    // ── Loop phases (from aiOS) ──
   241→    PhaseEntered {
   242→        phase: LoopPhase,
   243→    },
   244→    DeliberationProposed {
   245→        summary: String,
   246→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   247→        proposed_tool: Option<String>,
   248→    },
   249→
   250→    // ── Run lifecycle (from Lago + Arcan) ──
   251→    RunStarted {
   252→        provider: String,
   253→        max_iterations: u32,
   254→    },
   255→    RunFinished {
   256→        reason: String,
   257→        total_iterations: u32,
   258→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   259→        final_answer: Option<String>,
   260→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   261→        usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   262→    },
   263→    RunErrored {
   264→        error: String,
   265→    },
   266→
   267→    // ── Step lifecycle (from Lago) ──
   268→    StepStarted {
   269→        index: u32,
   270→    },
   271→    StepFinished {
   272→        index: u32,
   273→        stop_reason: String,
   274→        directive_count: usize,
   275→    },
   276→
   277→    // ── Text streaming (from Arcan + Lago) ──
   278→    AssistantTextDelta {
   279→        delta: String,
   280→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   281→        index: Option<u32>,
   282→    },
   283→    AssistantMessageCommitted {
   284→        role: String,
   285→        content: String,
   286→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   287→        model: Option<String>,
   288→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   289→        token_usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   290→    },
   291→    TextDelta {
   292→        delta: String,
   293→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   294→        index: Option<u32>,
   295→    },
   296→    Message {
   297→        role: String,
   298→        content: String,
   299→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   300→        model: Option<String>,
   301→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   302→        token_usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   303→    },
   304→
   305→    // ── Tool lifecycle (merged from all three) ──
   306→    ToolCallRequested {
   307→        call_id: String,
   308→        tool_name: String,
   309→        arguments: serde_json::Value,
   310→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   311→        category: Option<String>,
   312→    },
   313→    ToolCallStarted {
   314→        tool_run_id: ToolRunId,
   315→        tool_name: String,
   316→    },
   317→    ToolCallCompleted {
   318→        tool_run_id: ToolRunId,
   319→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   320→        call_id: Option<String>,
   321→        tool_name: String,
   322→        result: serde_json::Value,
   323→        duration_ms: u64,
   324→        status: SpanStatus,
   325→    },
   326→    ToolCallFailed {
   327→        call_id: String,
   328→        tool_name: String,
   329→        error: String,
   330→    },
   331→
   332→    // ── File operations (from Lago) ──
   333→    FileWrite {
   334→        path: String,
   335→        blob_hash: BlobHash,
   336→        size_bytes: u64,
   337→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   338→        content_type: Option<String>,
   339→    },
   340→    FileDelete {
   341→        path: String,
   342→    },
   343→    FileRename {
   344→        old_path: String,
   345→        new_path: String,
   346→    },
   347→    FileMutated {
   348→        path: String,
   349→        content_hash: String,
   350→    },
   351→
   352→    // ── State management (from Lago + Arcan) ──
   353→    StatePatchCommitted {
   354→        new_version: u64,
   355→        patch: StatePatch,
   356→    },
   357→    StatePatched {
   358→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   359→        index: Option<u32>,
   360→        patch: serde_json::Value,
   361→        revision: u64,
   362→    },
   363→    ContextCompacted {
   364→        dropped_count: usize,
   365→        tokens_before: usize,
   366→        tokens_after: usize,
   367→    },
   368→
   369→    // ── Policy (from Lago) ──
   370→    PolicyEvaluated {
   371→        tool_name: String,
   372→        decision: PolicyDecisionKind,
   373→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   374→        rule_id: Option<String>,
   375→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   376→        explanation: Option<String>,
   377→    },
   378→
   379→    // ── Approval gate (from Lago + Arcan + aiOS) ──
   380→    ApprovalRequested {
   381→        approval_id: ApprovalId,
   382→        call_id: String,
   383→        tool_name: String,
   384→        arguments: serde_json::Value,
   385→        risk: RiskLevel,
   386→    },
   387→    ApprovalResolved {
   388→        approval_id: ApprovalId,
   389→        decision: ApprovalDecision,
   390→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   391→        reason: Option<String>,
   392→    },
   393→
   394→    // ── Snapshots (from Lago) ──
   395→    SnapshotCreated {
   396→        snapshot_id: SnapshotId,
   397→        snapshot_type: SnapshotType,
   398→        covers_through_seq: SeqNo,
   399→        data_hash: BlobHash,
   400→    },
   401→
   402→    // ── Sandbox lifecycle (from Lago) ──
   403→    SandboxCreated {
   404→        sandbox_id: String,
   405→        tier: String,
   406→        config: serde_json::Value,
   407→    },
   408→    SandboxExecuted {
   409→        sandbox_id: String,
   410→        command: String,
   411→        exit_code: i32,
   412→        duration_ms: u64,
   413→    },
   414→    SandboxViolation {
   415→        sandbox_id: String,
   416→        violation_type: String,
   417→        details: String,
   418→    },
   419→    SandboxDestroyed {
   420→        sandbox_id: String,
   421→    },
   422→
   423→    // ── Memory (from Lago) ──
   424→    ObservationAppended {
   425→        scope: MemoryScope,
   426→        observation_ref: BlobHash,
   427→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   428→        source_run_id: Option<String>,
   429→    },
   430→    ReflectionCompacted {
   431→        scope: MemoryScope,
   432→        summary_ref: BlobHash,
   433→        covers_through_seq: SeqNo,
   434→    },
   435→    MemoryProposed {
   436→        scope: MemoryScope,
   437→        proposal_id: MemoryId,
   438→        entries_ref: BlobHash,
   439→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   440→        source_run_id: Option<String>,
   441→    },
   442→    MemoryCommitted {
   443→        scope: MemoryScope,
   444→        memory_id: MemoryId,
   445→        committed_ref: BlobHash,
   446→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   447→        supersedes: Option<MemoryId>,
   448→    },
   449→    MemoryTombstoned {
   450→        scope: MemoryScope,
   451→        memory_id: MemoryId,
   452→        reason: String,
   453→    },
   454→
   455→    // ── Homeostasis (from aiOS) ──
   456→    Heartbeat {
   457→        summary: String,
   458→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   459→        checkpoint_id: Option<CheckpointId>,
   460→    },
   461→    StateEstimated {
   462→        state: AgentStateVector,
   463→        mode: OperatingMode,
   464→    },
   465→    BudgetUpdated {
   466→        budget: BudgetState,
   467→        reason: String,
   468→    },
   469→    ModeChanged {
   470→        from: OperatingMode,
   471→        to: OperatingMode,
   472→        reason: String,
   473→    },
   474→    GatesUpdated {
   475→        gates: serde_json::Value,
   476→        reason: String,
   477→    },
   478→    CircuitBreakerTripped {
   479→        reason: String,
   480→        error_streak: u32,
   481→    },
   482→
   483→    // ── Checkpoints (from aiOS) ──
   484→    CheckpointCreated {
   485→        checkpoint_id: CheckpointId,
   486→        event_sequence: u64,
   487→        state_hash: String,
   488→    },
   489→    CheckpointRestored {
   490→        checkpoint_id: CheckpointId,
   491→        restored_to_seq: u64,
   492→    },
   493→
   494→    // ── Voice (from aiOS) ──
   495→    VoiceSessionStarted {
   496→        voice_session_id: String,
   497→        adapter: String,
   498→        model: String,
   499→        sample_rate_hz: u32,
   500→        channels: u8,
   501→    },
   502→    VoiceInputChunk {
   503→        voice_session_id: String,
   504→        chunk_index: u64,
   505→        bytes: usize,
   506→        format: String,
   507→    },
   508→    VoiceOutputChunk {
   509→        voice_session_id: String,
   510→        chunk_index: u64,
   511→        bytes: usize,
   512→        format: String,
   513→    },
   514→    VoiceSessionStopped {
   515→        voice_session_id: String,
   516→        reason: String,
   517→    },
   518→    VoiceAdapterError {
   519→        voice_session_id: String,
   520→        message: String,
   521→    },
   522→
   523→    // ── World models (new, forward-looking) ──
   524→    WorldModelObserved {
   525→        state_ref: BlobHash,
   526→        meta: serde_json::Value,
   527→    },
   528→    WorldModelRollout {
   529→        trajectory_ref: BlobHash,
   530→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   531→        score: Option<f32>,
   532→    },
   533→
   534→    // ── Intent lifecycle (new) ──
   535→    IntentProposed {
   536→        intent_id: String,
   537→        kind: String,

   971→/// Forward-compatible deserializer: unknown variants become `Custom`.
   972→impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for EventKind {
   973→    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
   974→    where
   975→        D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
   976→    {
   977→        let raw = serde_json::Value::deserialize(deserializer)?;
   978→        match serde_json::from_value::<EventKindKnown>(raw.clone()) {
   979→            Ok(known) => Ok(known.into()),
   980→            Err(_) => {
   981→                let event_type = raw
   982→                    .get("type")
   983→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   984→                    .unwrap_or("Unknown")
   985→                    .to_string();
   986→                let mut data = raw;
   987→                if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {
   988→                    obj.remove("type");
   989→                }
   990→                Ok(EventKind::Custom { event_type, data })
   991→            }
   992→        }
   993→    }
   994→}

   996→/// Conversion from the known helper enum to the public EventKind.
   997→/// This is mechanical — each variant maps 1:1.
   998→impl From<EventKindKnown> for EventKind {
   999→    #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
  1000→    fn from(k: EventKindKnown) -> Self {
  1001→        match k {
  1002→            EventKindKnown::UserMessage { content } => Self::UserMessage { content },
  1003→            EventKindKnown::ExternalSignal { signal_type, data } => {
  1004→                Self::ExternalSignal { signal_type, data }
  1005→            }
  1006→            EventKindKnown::SessionCreated { name, config } => {
  1007→                Self::SessionCreated { name, config }

  1009→            EventKindKnown::SessionResumed { from_snapshot } => {
  1010→                Self::SessionResumed { from_snapshot }

  1012→            EventKindKnown::SessionClosed { reason } => Self::SessionClosed { reason },
  1013→            EventKindKnown::BranchCreated {

  1018→                new_branch_id,
  1019→                fork_point_seq,
  1020→                name,

  1427→#[cfg(test)]
  1428→mod tests {
  1429→    use super::*;
  1430→
  1431→    fn make_envelope(kind: EventKind) -> EventEnvelope {
  1432→        EventEnvelope {
  1433→            event_id: EventId::from_string("EVT001"),
  1434→            session_id: SessionId::from_string("SESS001"),
  1435→            agent_id: AgentId::from_string("AGENT001"),
  1436→            branch_id: BranchId::from_string("main"),
  1437→            run_id: None,
  1438→            seq: 1,
  1439→            timestamp: 1_700_000_000_000_000,
  1440→            actor: EventActor::default(),
  1441→            schema: EventSchema::default(),
  1442→            parent_id: None,
  1443→            trace_id: None,
  1444→            span_id: None,
  1445→            digest: None,
  1446→            kind,
  1447→            metadata: HashMap::new(),
  1448→            schema_version: 1,
  1449→        }
  1450→    }
  1451→
  1452→    #[test]
  1453→    fn error_raised_roundtrip() {
  1454→        let kind = EventKind::ErrorRaised {
  1455→            message: "boom".into(),
  1456→        };
  1457→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
  1458→        assert!(json.contains("\"type\":\"ErrorRaised\""));
  1459→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1460→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::ErrorRaised { message } if message == "boom"));
  1461→    }
  1462→
  1463→    #[test]
  1464→    fn heartbeat_roundtrip() {
  1465→        let kind = EventKind::Heartbeat {
  1466→            summary: "alive".into(),
  1467→            checkpoint_id: None,
  1468→        };
  1469→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
  1470→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1471→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::Heartbeat { .. }));
  1472→    }
  1473→
  1474→    #[test]
  1475→    fn state_estimated_roundtrip() {
  1476→        let kind = EventKind::StateEstimated {
  1477→            state: AgentStateVector::default(),
  1478→            mode: OperatingMode::Execute,
  1479→        };
  1480→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
  1481→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1482→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::StateEstimated { .. }));
  1483→    }
  1484→
  1485→    #[test]
  1486→    fn unknown_variant_becomes_custom() {
  1487→        let json = r#"{"type":"FutureFeature","key":"value","num":42}"#;
  1488→        let kind: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(json).unwrap();
  1489→        if let EventKind::Custom { event_type, data } = kind {
  1490→            assert_eq!(event_type, "FutureFeature");
  1491→            assert_eq!(data["key"], "value");
  1492→            assert_eq!(data["num"], 42);
  1493→        } else {
  1494→            panic!("should be Custom");
  1495→        }
  1496→    }
  1497→
  1498→    #[test]
  1499→    fn full_envelope_roundtrip() {
  1500→        let envelope = make_envelope(EventKind::RunStarted {
  1501→            provider: "anthropic".into(),
  1502→            max_iterations: 10,
  1503→        });
  1504→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&envelope).unwrap();
  1505→        let back: EventEnvelope = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1506→        assert_eq!(back.seq, 1);
  1507→        assert_eq!(back.schema_version, 1);
  1508→        assert!(matches!(back.kind, EventKind::RunStarted { .. }));
  1509→    }
  1510→
  1511→    #[test]
  1512→    fn tool_call_lifecycle_roundtrip() {
  1513→        let requested = EventKind::ToolCallRequested {
  1514→            call_id: "c1".into(),
  1515→            tool_name: "read_file".into(),
  1516→            arguments: serde_json::json!({"path": "/etc/hosts"}),
  1517→            category: Some("fs".into()),
  1518→        };
  1519→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&requested).unwrap();
  1520→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1521→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::ToolCallRequested { .. }));
  1522→    }
  1523→
  1524→    #[test]
  1525→    fn memory_events_roundtrip() {
  1526→        let proposed = EventKind::MemoryProposed {
  1527→            scope: MemoryScope::Agent,
  1528→            proposal_id: MemoryId::from_string("PROP001"),
  1529→            entries_ref: BlobHash::from_hex("abc"),
  1530→            source_run_id: None,
  1531→        };
  1532→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&proposed).unwrap();
  1533→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1534→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::MemoryProposed { .. }));
  1535→    }
  1536→
  1537→    #[test]
  1538→    fn mode_changed_roundtrip() {
  1539→        let kind = EventKind::ModeChanged {
  1540→            from: OperatingMode::Execute,
  1541→            to: OperatingMode::Recover,
  1542→            reason: "error streak".into(),
  1543→        };
  1544→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
  1545→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1546→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::ModeChanged { .. }));
  1547→    }
  1548→
  1549→    #[test]
  1550→    fn schema_version_defaults_to_1() {
  1551→        let json = r#"{"event_id":"E1","session_id":"S1","branch_id":"main","seq":0,"timestamp":100,"kind":{"type":"ErrorRaised","message":"x"},"metadata":{}}"#;
  1552→        let envelope: EventEnvelope = serde_json::from_str(json).unwrap();
  1553→        assert_eq!(envelope.schema_version, 1);
  1554→    }
  1555→
  1556→    #[test]
  1557→    fn voice_events_roundtrip() {
  1558→        let kind = EventKind::VoiceSessionStarted {
  1559→            voice_session_id: "vs1".into(),
  1560→            adapter: "openai-realtime".into(),
  1561→            model: "gpt-4o-realtime".into(),
  1562→            sample_rate_hz: 24000,
  1563→            channels: 1,
  1564→        };
  1565→        let json = serde_json::to_string(&kind).unwrap();
  1566→        let back: EventKind = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();
  1567→        assert!(matches!(back, EventKind::VoiceSessionStarted { .. }));
  1568→    }
  1569→}

content.text:
Thoroughly explore the Lago event-sourced persistence substrate to understand how Autonomic will subscribe to events and write back decisions. I need to understand:

1. **Event journal** - How events are stored, the Journal trait, event types
2. **SSE streaming** - How Lago exposes event streams via SSE (look in lago-api)
3. **lago-core types** - The core event types, event envelope structure
4. **Ingest system** - How events are ingested (lago-ingest)
5. **The arcan-lago bridge** - How Arcan currently connects to Lago, what patterns autonomic-lago should follow
6. **lago-aios-eventstore-adapter** - How the canonical EventStorePort is implemented

Search in `lago/` and `arcan/arcan-lago/` directories. Read full files for:
- lago-core/src/lib.rs and all type files
- lago-api SSE-related files
- lago-journal trait definition
- arcan-lago bridge implementation
- lago-aios-eventstore-adapter

I need the complete picture of the event flow: how events enter Lago, how they're stored, and how they can be subscribed to via SSE.

content.content:
1→use lago_core::EventEnvelope;
     2→
     3→/// A single frame to be sent over the SSE connection.
     4→pub struct SseFrame {
     5→    /// Optional event type (the `event:` field in SSE).
     6→    pub event: Option<String>,
     7→    /// The `data:` field in SSE (JSON-encoded string, typically).
     8→    pub data: String,
     9→    /// Optional event ID (the `id:` field in SSE). Used for reconnection.
    10→    pub id: Option<String>,
    11→}
    12→
    13→/// Trait for formatting `EventEnvelope`s into SSE frames for a given wire
    14→/// protocol (OpenAI, Anthropic, Vercel AI SDK, or native Lago format).
    15→pub trait SseFormat: Send + Sync {
    16→    /// Convert an event envelope into zero or more SSE frames.
    17→    ///
    18→    /// Returning an empty vec means the event is filtered out (not relevant
    19→    /// to this format).
    20→    fn format(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Vec<SseFrame>;
    21→
    22→    /// An optional "done" frame to send when the stream terminates.
    23→    fn done_frame(&self) -> Option<SseFrame>;
    24→
    25→    /// Extra HTTP headers to include in the SSE response (e.g. Vercel's
    26→    /// `x-vercel-ai-data-stream` header).
    27→    fn extra_headers(&self) -> Vec<(String, String)>;
    28→
    29→    /// Human-readable name of this format (for logging).
    30→    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    31→}
    32→

1→# Agent OS: Forward Roadmap (Post-Baseline)
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-02-22  
     4→**Baseline**: Canonical runtime unification complete
     5→
     6→This roadmap starts from the active canonical baseline and lists forward-only execution phases.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 0) Baseline Snapshot
    11→
    12→Completed baseline outcomes:
    13→
    14→- `aios-protocol` is active contract boundary.
    15→- `aios-runtime` is active runtime engine in production host path.
    16→- Lago-backed canonical event-store adapter is active.
    17→- Arcan adapter layer is active.
    18→- Canonical session API family is active and tested.
    19→- Dependency architecture audit is integrated in control audit.
    20→- Conformance harness is green.
    21→
    22→Baseline is now the required foundation for all new work.
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## 1) Phase R1 — Conformance Expansion
    27→
    28→**Goal**: Raise confidence from baseline correctness to stronger determinism guarantees across projects.
    29→
    30→### Scope
    31→
    32→1. Expand cross-project golden fixtures for event replay determinism.
    33→2. Add protocol forward-compat fixtures for unknown/custom event evolution.
    34→3. Add stronger stream cursor/replay invariants in canonical API tests.
    35→4. Add branch-heavy replay/merge deterministic fixtures.
    36→
    37→### Acceptance
    38→
    39→- Golden fixture suite covers at least canonical run lifecycle, branch lifecycle, approval flow, and replay parity across adapters.
    40→- Determinism checks pass in CI/conformance without flaky tolerance.
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## 2) Phase R2 — Observability Maturity
    45→
    46→**Goal**: Make runtime and adapter behavior operationally transparent.
    47→
    48→### Scope
    49→
    50→1. Expand structured telemetry across canonical runtime phases and adapter boundaries.
    51→2. Add clear metrics for run throughput, failure classes, approval latency, and stream health.
    52→3. Improve auditability of canonical event write/read paths.
    53→4. Add operator-facing diagnostics for branch/replay anomalies.
    54→
    55→### Acceptance
    56→
    57→- Key runtime and adapter paths emit structured telemetry with stable field shapes.
    58→- Alertable signals exist for failure hotspots and stream/persistence health regressions.
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→## 3) Phase R3 — Security Hardening
    63→
    64→**Goal**: Move from baseline-safe behavior to hardened runtime controls.
    65→
    66→### Scope
    67→
    68→1. Strengthen sandbox isolation beyond current process-level controls.
    69→2. Tighten capability and approval policy defaults where appropriate.
    70→3. Expand secret-handling and redaction coverage in logs/events.
    71→4. Add explicit tests for high-risk capability gating paths.
    72→
    73→### Acceptance
    74→
    75→- Hardened sandbox policy profile is available and verified in test gates.
    76→- Security-sensitive path tests pass in workspace gates.
    77→
    78→---
    79→
    80→## 4) Phase R4 — Memory and Learning Depth
    81→
    82→**Goal**: Evolve memory/learning features on top of canonical baseline without violating contract boundaries.
    83→
    84→### Scope
    85→
    86→1. Expand canonical memory workflows (proposal/commit/query quality and policies).
    87→2. Improve event-derived memory projection/query fidelity.
    88→3. Add learning-oriented fixture and regression suites for memory lifecycle events.
    89→4. Keep all enhancements contract-first through `aios-protocol`.
    90→
    91→### Acceptance
    92→
    93→- Memory lifecycle semantics remain replay-safe and deterministic.
    94→- Memory behavior remains adapter-agnostic at runtime boundary.
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## 5) Phase R5 — Controller Plane (Autonomic)
    99→
   100→**Goal**: Introduce controller-driven stability behaviors without creating a second runtime model.
   101→
   102→### Scope
   103→
   104→1. Define minimal controller integration contract using canonical protocol boundaries.
   105→2. Implement controller outputs as policy/gating inputs into canonical runtime path.
   106→3. Ensure controller actions are event-auditable and replay-safe.
   107→
   108→### Acceptance
   109→
   110→- Controller integration does not bypass canonical runtime/event paths.
   111→- Controller-induced decisions are visible and deterministic in replay.
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→## 6) Ongoing Engineering Rules
   116→
   117→These rules apply to every roadmap phase:
   118→
   119→1. Contract-first changes: boundary updates land in `aios-protocol` first.
   120→2. No alternate production runtime path.
   121→3. No cross-layer concrete dependency violations.
   122→4. Every phase must pass build/lint/test/conformance/audit gates.
   123→5. Documentation must be updated in the same change set as architecture behavior changes.
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## 7) Required Gates Per Milestone
   128→
   129→A milestone is complete only when all pass:
   130→
   131→1. `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/aiOS`
   132→   - `cargo fmt`
   133→   - `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`
   134→   - `cargo test --workspace`
   135→2. `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/arcan`
   136→   - `cargo fmt`
   137→   - `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`
   138→   - `cargo test --workspace`
   139→3. `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/lago`
   140→   - `cargo fmt`
   141→   - `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`
   142→   - `cargo test --workspace`
   143→4. `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live`
   144→   - `make audit`
   145→   - `./conformance/run.sh`
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## 8) Tracking Model
   150→
   151→Status labels used in this roadmap:
   152→
   153→- `COMPLETE`: implemented and gate-validated.
   154→- `ACTIVE`: currently in execution.
   155→- `PLANNED`: approved but not started.
   156→- `BLOCKED`: cannot proceed due to unmet dependency/gate.
   157→
   158→Current labels:
   159→
   160→- Baseline unification: `COMPLETE`
   161→- R1 Conformance expansion: `ACTIVE`
   162→- R2 Observability maturity: `PLANNED`
   163→- R3 Security hardening: `PLANNED`
   164→- R4 Memory/learning depth: `PLANNED`
   165→- R5 Controller plane: `PLANNED`
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→## 9) Planned Feature Track: Economic Actuation (Conway-Compatible)
   170→
   171→Status: `PLANNED`
   172→
   173→Reference:
   174→- `docs/FEATURE_CONWAY_ACTUATION.md`
   175→
   176→Intent:
   177→- Add contract-safe economic/payment and external provisioning primitives (payment events, resource lease lifecycle, capability gates, spend-aware homeostasis) while preserving canonical replay and policy guarantees.
   178→
   179→Placement in roadmap:
   180→- Cross-cuts R2/R3/R5.
   181→- Must not bypass canonical runtime or event paths.
   182→
   183→

1→# Agent OS: Canonical Architecture
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-02-22  
     4→**Baseline**: Canonical runtime unification active
     5→
     6→This document describes the active architecture in `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live`.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1) System Overview
    11→
    12→The system is a contract-first architecture across three active projects (plus one planned controller project):
    13→
    14→- **aiOS**: canonical contract + runtime engine
    15→- **Arcan**: daemon host + adapters + clients
    16→- **Lago**: durable event-sourced persistence substrate
    17→- **Autonomic** (planned): controller for advanced homeostasis/maintenance policies
    18→
    19→### Active Baseline Spine
    20→
    21→1. `aios-protocol` defines canonical runtime contract and boundary types.
    22→2. `aios-runtime` executes runtime behavior through protocol ports.
    23→3. Lago persistence is consumed through canonical adapter implementation.
    24→4. Arcan hosts the runtime and provides adapter implementations for provider/harness/policy/approval/memory.
    25→5. Runtime API is the canonical session API family.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## 2) Canonical Boundaries
    30→
    31→## Contract Boundary
    32→
    33→- Boundary crate: `aios-protocol`
    34→- Canonical boundary types include:
    35→  - `EventRecord`, `EventKind`
    36→  - protocol IDs (`SessionId`, `BranchId`, `RunId`, etc.)
    37→  - canonical state structures
    38→
    39→## Runtime Ports
    40→
    41→`aios-protocol` runtime ports:
    42→
    43→- `EventStorePort`
    44→- `ModelProviderPort`
    45→- `ToolHarnessPort`
    46→- `PolicyGatePort`
    47→- `ApprovalPort`
    48→- `MemoryPort`
    49→
    50→`aios-runtime` depends only on these ports and canonical protocol types.
    51→
    52→## Dependency Invariants
    53→
    54→1. aiOS core crates must not depend on Arcan/Lago implementation crates.
    55→2. Lago core crates must not depend on Arcan crates.
    56→3. Runtime path data exchange must remain canonical protocol types.
    57→4. Architecture dependency edges are validated by audit gate scripts.
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## 3) Runtime Host Topology
    62→
    63→## Canonical Runtime Host (Arcan)
    64→
    65→`arcan` daemon composes:
    66→
    67→- `aios-runtime::KernelRuntime`
    68→- Lago-backed canonical event store adapter
    69→- Arcan adapter implementations for provider/tools/policy/approval/memory
    70→- `arcand::canonical` router
    71→
    72→## Canonical API Surface
    73→
    74→- `POST /sessions`
    75→- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/runs`
    76→- `GET /sessions/{session_id}/state`
    77→- `GET /sessions/{session_id}/events`
    78→- `GET /sessions/{session_id}/events/stream`
    79→- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/branches`
    80→- `GET /sessions/{session_id}/branches`
    81→- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/branches/{branch_id}/merge`
    82→- `POST /sessions/{session_id}/approvals/{approval_id}`
    83→
    84→No alternate production runtime route family is part of the baseline.
    85→
    86→---
    87→
    88→## 4) Persistence Topology (Lago)
    89→
    90→## Event Journal
    91→
    92→Lago provides append-only journal semantics with branch-local monotonic sequencing.
    93→
    94→Key properties in active runtime path:
    95→
    96→- append/read/head semantics through canonical adapter implementation
    97→- branch-aware sequence assignment
    98→- replay-compatible event access
    99→
   100→## Blob + Store + Policy
   101→
   102→Lago substrate provides:
   103→
   104→- content-addressed blob storage
   105→- filesystem/manifest capabilities
   106→- policy engine support
   107→- API and stream formatting utilities used by integration layers
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## 5) Adapter Architecture
   112→
   113→## Lago Adapter
   114→
   115→- Crate: `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter`
   116→- Responsibility: implement `EventStorePort` over `lago_core::Journal`
   117→- Conversion path: canonical bridge conversions via Lago core protocol bridge
   118→
   119→## Arcan Adapters
   120→
   121→- Crate: `arcan-aios-adapters`
   122→- Responsibilities:
   123→  - model provider adapter
   124→  - tool harness adapter
   125→  - policy gate adapter
   126→  - approval adapter
   127→  - memory adapter
   128→
   129→Adapters isolate implementation details from canonical runtime contract.
   130→
   131→---
   132→
   133→## 6) Runtime Data Flow
   134→
   135→Canonical run flow:
   136→
   137→1. Client requests session creation or run execution.
   138→2. Host ensures canonical session state is available.
   139→3. `aios-runtime` executes run loop through port interfaces.
   140→4. Events are appended/read through canonical event-store port implementation.
   141→5. State and lifecycle updates are emitted as canonical events.
   142→6. Clients consume event replay or event stream through canonical endpoints.
   143→
   144→Branch flow:
   145→
   146→- branch create/list/merge operations are handled through canonical runtime APIs and persisted through canonical event storage path.
   147→
   148→Approval flow:
   149→
   150→- approval resolution uses canonical approval endpoint and canonical runtime approval port.
   151→
   152→---
   153→
   154→## 7) Streaming Model
   155→
   156→Primary stream endpoint:
   157→
   158→- `GET /sessions/{session_id}/events/stream`
   159→
   160→Supported behavior:
   161→
   162→- canonical event streaming for replay/live consumption
   163→- optional Vercel AI SDK v6 envelope path through format handling in canonical stream route
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→## 8) Governance and Enforcement
   168→
   169→Architecture enforcement is integrated into control audit:
   170→
   171→- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/scripts/architecture/verify_dependencies.sh`
   172→- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/Makefile.control`
   173→- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/scripts/audit_control.sh`
   174→
   175→Conformance and integration gates are exercised by:
   176→
   177→- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/conformance/run.sh`
   178→
   179→---
   180→
   181→## 9) Crate Role Map (Active)
   182→
   183→## aiOS
   184→
   185→- `aios-protocol`: canonical contract and runtime ports
   186→- `aios-runtime`: runtime engine
   187→- `aios-kernel`: composition layer
   188→- `aios-events` / `aios-policy` / `aios-memory` / `aios-tools`: canonical port-aligned components
   189→
   190→## Arcan
   191→
   192→- `arcan`: daemon host binary
   193→- `arcand`: canonical session API router
   194→- `arcan-aios-adapters`: canonical port adapter implementations
   195→- `arcan-core`, `arcan-harness`, `arcan-provider`, `arcan-store`, `arcan-lago`, `arcan-tui`: supporting runtime/client layers
   196→
   197→## Lago
   198→
   199→- `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter`: canonical event-store adapter
   200→- `lago-core`, `lago-journal`, `lago-store`, `lago-fs`, `lago-policy`, `lago-api`, `lago-ingest`, `lagod`, `lago-cli`: persistence substrate stack
   201→
   202→---
   203→
   204→## 10) Current Constraints
   205→
   206→1. Baseline emphasizes canonical runtime/persistence integration, not full observability maturity.
   207→2. OS-level sandbox hardening remains an active follow-up area.
   208→3. Cross-project golden fixture breadth can still be expanded.
   209→4. Additional controller-plane capabilities (Autonomic) remain planned.
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## 11) Definition of Architectural Baseline
   214→
   215→The baseline is complete when all of the following hold (currently true):
   216→
   217→- Canonical contract is the sole integration contract.
   218→- Canonical runtime engine is the production runtime path.
   219→- Lago is active persistence backend for runtime events through canonical adapter path.
   220→- Canonical session API is the production runtime API family.
   221→- Architecture dependency audit and conformance gates pass.
   222→
   223→

content.content:
1→# Arcan + Lago: Consolidated Development Plan
     2→
     3→**Last updated**: 2026-02-28
     4→**Version**: 0.2.0
     5→
     6→## Progress Tracker
     7→
     8→| Phase | Milestone | Status | Tests Added |
     9→|-------|-----------|--------|-------------|
    10→| 0.1 | Context Window Management | DONE | +11 |
    11→| 0.2 | Integration Test Infrastructure | DONE | +18 (10 arcand + 8 lago) |
    12→| 0 | Stabilization & Test Coverage | **DONE** | +4 (1 policy test, 3 E2E integration) |
    13→| 1 | Memory & Context Compiler | **DONE** | +82 |
    14→| 2.1 | Learning Capture | DONE | +8 |
    15→| 2.4 | Heartbeat Scheduler | DONE | +9 |
    16→| 2.6 | Approval Workflow | DONE | +14 |
    17→| 2 | Self-Learning & Heartbeats (remaining: 2.2, 2.3, 2.5) | Planned | target: +23 |
    18→| P1 | AI SDK v6 UI Message Stream Protocol | **DONE** | +10 (18 unit + 5 integration, net of replaced v5 tests) |
    19→| P2.2 | OpenAI + Ollama Provider + Retry Logic | **DONE** | +13 |
    20→| P2 | Safety & Multi-Provider (remaining: edits, sandbox) | Planned | target: +25 |
    21→| P3 | Session Management & Clients | Planned | target: +24 |
    22→| P4 | Advanced Runtime (subagents, web client, WASM) | Future | — |
    23→
    24→**Current test counts**: aiOS 61 passing, Arcan 272 passing (+1 ignored), Lago 324 passing, Total 657 passing (+1 ignored)
    25→**Target**: 700+ tests by end of Phase 5
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Current State Assessment
    30→
    31→**What works end-to-end**: POST /chat -> agent loop -> tools -> Lago journal -> SSE stream.
    32→Core event sourcing, blob storage, FS branching, policy engine, gRPC ingest, multi-format SSE
    33→are all implemented and tested across 19 crates (~27.8K lines Rust).
    34→
    35→**Critical gaps vs the vision** (from `docs/raw-dumps/lago-lakehouse-memory.md`):
    36→1. **Memory**: Agents have basic key-value memory only; no observational memory, semantic recall, or graph memory
    37→2. **Context compiler**: No deterministic assembly of persona + rules + memory + retrieval + workspace
    38→3. **Self-learning**: No capture -> consolidate -> promote pipeline with governance gates
    39→4. **Skills as Lago artifacts**: Skills discovered from local FS, not versioned in Lago
    40→5. **Observability**: Basic tracing only, no OTel + GenAI semantic conventions
    41→6. ~~**Operational tooling**: CLI stubs, no replay debugger~~ CLI fully implemented; replay debugger still needed
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## Phase 0: Stabilization & Test Coverage — COMPLETE
    46→
    47→**Goal**: Fix what's broken, establish integration test infrastructure, wire unused components.
    48→
    49→### 0.1 Fix Lago E2E Tests — DONE (already passing)
    50→- E2E tests were already fixed and compiling. 25 E2E tests pass.
    51→
    52→### 0.2 Wire Blob Storage to File Operations — DONE (already wired)
    53→- Blob storage was already fully wired: `write_file` → `blob_store.put()`, `read_file` → `blob_store.get()`, `patch_file` reads/writes through blob store
    54→- 17 E2E tests confirm round-trip through blob storage
    55→
    56→### 0.3 Complete lago-cli Command Handlers — DONE (already implemented)
    57→- All command handlers were already fully implemented:
    58→  - `session` (create/list/show with API client + DB fallback)
    59→  - `log` (comprehensive formatting for all EventPayload variants)
    60→  - `cat` (manifest reconstruction → blob fetch → stdout)
    61→  - `branch` (create with fork_at support + formatted list)
    62→
    63→### 0.4 Create Default Policy Rules — DONE
    64→- Created `default-policy.toml` with 5 rules, 3 roles, 2 hooks
    65→- `lago init` now scaffolds `policy.toml` alongside `lago.toml`
    66→- `lagod` loads policy on startup when file exists
    67→- Compile-time test verifies the default policy file parses correctly
    68→
    69→### 0.5 Cross-Project Integration Test — DONE
    70→- Added `arcan-lago/tests/end_to_end.rs` with 3 comprehensive tests:
    71→  - `full_session_lifecycle_round_trip`: 9 events → journal → projection → verify state+history
    72→  - `policy_middleware_with_default_rules`: deny-shell, allow-filesystem, default-allow
    73→  - `multiple_sessions_isolated`: concurrent sessions share journal without interference
    74→
    75→### 0.6 Cross-Project Integration Test
    76→- Full round-trip: arcan POST /chat -> lago journal -> verify event sequence
    77→- Session replay: create session -> reload -> verify state matches
    78→
    79→### 0.5 Default Policy Rules
    80→- Create `default-policy.toml` with baseline rules (deny dangerous patterns, require approval for destructive ops)
    81→- Load at Arcan startup via `--policy` flag
    82→
    83→**Validation**: Both projects pass all tests. CLI usable for inspection. Blob storage wired.
    84→
    85→---
    86→
    87→## Phase 1: Memory & Context Compiler — COMPLETE
    88→
    89→**Goal**: Agents remember across sessions and receive intelligently assembled context.
    90→This is the highest-leverage phase — everything else depends on memory working.
    91→
    92→**Result**: +57 tests (415 → 472). All milestones complete, zero warnings.
    93→
    94→### 1.1 Memory Event Types in Lago — DONE
    95→Added 5 new `EventPayload` variants to `lago-core`:
    96→- `ObservationAppended { scope, observation_ref, source_run_id }`
    97→- `ReflectionCompacted { scope, summary_ref, covers_through_seq }`
    98→- `MemoryProposed { scope, proposal_id, entries_ref, source_run_id }`
    99→- `MemoryCommitted { scope, memory_id, committed_ref, supersedes }`
   100→- `MemoryTombstoned { scope, memory_id, reason }`
   101→
   102→Plus `MemoryScope` enum (Session/User/Agent/Org), `MemoryId` typed ID. 13 new tests in lago-core.
   103→
   104→### 1.2 Observational Memory Baseline — DONE
   105→Created in `arcan-lago`:
   106→- `Observer` (implements `Projection`) — extracts observations from Message, ToolResult, Error, StatePatched events
   107→- `Reflector` — text-based compaction (no LLM call needed)
   108→- `MemoryProjection` (implements `Projection`) — queryable memory state from events, handles tombstones and supersedes
   109→- `MemoryScopeConfig` + `MemoryEntry` — runtime configuration and entry types
   110→20 new tests across observation.rs, memory_projection.rs, memory_scope.rs.
   111→
   112→### 1.3 Context Compiler — DONE
   113→Created `context_compiler.rs` in `arcan-core`:
   114→- `ContextBlockKind`: Persona, Rules, Memory, Retrieval, Workspace, Task
   115→- `compile_context()`: fixed assembly order, per-block token budgets, priority-based overflow dropping, word-boundary truncation
   116→- Persona block never dropped (highest priority)
   117→- Wired into `OrchestratorConfig` as optional field
   118→14 new tests.
   119→
   120→### 1.4 Governed Memory Tools — DONE
   121→Created 3 `Tool` implementations in `arcan-lago`:
   122→- `MemoryQueryTool` — reads from `MemoryProjection` (Arc<RwLock>)
   123→- `MemoryProposeTool` — writes `MemoryProposed` event to Journal
   124→- `MemoryCommitTool` — writes `MemoryCommitted` event after policy check
   125→10 new tests using RedbJournal in tempdir.
   126→
   127→**Validation**: Agent has event-sourced, governed memory. Context assembly is structured and budget-aware.
   128→
   129→---
   130→
   131→## Phase 2: Self-Learning & Heartbeats (Weeks 6-7)
   132→
   133→**Goal**: Agents improve over time. Heartbeats enable maintenance.
   134→
   135→### 2.1 Learning Capture
   136→- Structured entries on tool failures, user corrections, repeated confusion
   137→- Written to `/learnings/{errors,learnings,features}.md` in Lago
   138→
   139→### 2.2 Consolidation Job
   140→- Dedupe/cluster learnings -> generate rule proposals
   141→- Proposals stored in Lago with provenance links: `/proposals/<id>/rules.patch`
   142→
   143→### 2.3 Promotion Gate
   144→- Policy-gated commit: `rules.commit(proposal_id)`
   145→- Only promoted rules take effect; full rollback possible
   146→- Human approval optional (configurable)
   147→
   148→### 2.4 Heartbeat Scheduler
   149→- `RunTrigger::Heartbeat` periodic turns
   150→- Cheap deterministic checks first (queue depth, stuck tools, disk quota)
   151→- Only call LLM if there's something to interpret
   152→- `HeartbeatOk` for silent no-op
   153→
   154→### 2.5 Queue & Steering Semantics
   155→- `collect` / `steer` / `followup` / `interrupt` modes
   156→- Steering at tool boundaries (safe preemption points)
   157→- Prevents concurrent state corruption
   158→
   159→### 2.6 Approval Workflow ✅ DONE (2026-02-15)
   160→- ~~`POST /approve` endpoint with approval queue~~ DONE
   161→- ~~`RequireApproval` pauses run (tokio oneshot), resumes on approval~~ DONE
   162→- ~~Auto-deny after configurable timeout (5min default)~~ DONE
   163→- `ApprovalGate` in arcan-lago with `ApprovalGateHook`/`ApprovalResolver` traits in arcan-core
   164→- `POST /approve` + `GET /approvals` HTTP endpoints in arcand
   165→- 14 new tests (7 gate, 2 event-map, 2 middleware, 3 HTTP)
   166→
   167→**Validation**: Agent learns from mistakes via governed pipeline. Heartbeats fire.
   168→Approval workflow is interactive.
   169→
   170→---
   171→
   172→## Phase 3: Skills as Lago Artifacts + Multi-Provider (Weeks 8-10)
   173→
   174→**Goal**: Skills are versioned, reproducible. Multiple LLM providers work.
   175→
   176→### 3.1 Skill Manifest Schema
   177→```json
   178→{
   179→  "skill_id": "...",
   180→  "version": "1.0.0",
   181→  "requires": { "capabilities": [...], "approvals": [...] },
   182→  "prompt_blocks": { "system": "prompts/system.md" },
   183→  "tools": "tools/schema.json",
   184→  "policy": "policies/allowlist.json"
   185→}
   186→```
   187→- Events: `SkillInstalled`, `SkillActivated`, `SkillRemoved`
   188→- Immutable versioned snapshots in Lago
   189→
   190→### 3.2 Skill Loader in Harness
   191→- Compile active skill set from Lago
   192→- Assemble prompt blocks into context compiler
   193→- Build tool registry from skill tool schemas
   194→- `LagoResolver` trait instead of `PathBuf` for all config loading
   195→
   196→### 3.3 Skills Ingest
   197→- Fetch bundle -> verify signature/hash -> store in Lago -> register events
   198→- Policy bind: map skill capabilities to Lago policy rules
   199→
   200→### 3.4 OpenAI Provider
   201→- Chat completions API with function calling -> ToolCall mapping
   202→- Fallback routing: Claude -> OpenAI (configurable)
   203→
   204→### 3.5 Provider Retry & Cost Tracking
   205→- Exponential backoff for 429/5xx/timeout
   206→- Circuit breaker on provider errors
   207→- Per-session token budgets; budget exceeded -> run stops
   208→
   209→**Validation**: Skills are Lago artifacts with full provenance. OpenAI works.
   210→Cost tracked per session.
   211→
   212→---
   213→
   214→## Phase 4: Observability & Operational Tooling (Weeks 11-13)
   215→
   216→### 4.1 OpenTelemetry Integration
   217→- Spans: run -> turn -> tool -> llm -> memory
   218→- `tracing-opentelemetry` + OTLP exporter
   219→
   220→### 4.2 GenAI Semantic Conventions
   221→- Token usage, model, prompt hash in span attributes
   222→- OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions for LLM spans
   223→
   224→### 4.3 Structured Audit Events
   225→- Who triggered what, what data touched
   226→- Queryable from Lago events
   227→
   228→### 4.4 Replay CLI
   229→- `lago replay <session>` reconstructs agent view at event N
   230→- `lago diff --from N --to M` shows state changes
   231→
   232→### 4.5 Streaming Boundary Signals
   233→- text-start/end, tool-start/end SSE events
   234→- SSE event IDs + `retry:` headers for reconnection
   235→
   236→**Validation**: Full distributed tracing. Replay works. AI SDK clients handle boundaries.
   237→
   238→---
   239→
   240→## Phase 5: Governance & Security Hardening (Weeks 14-16)
   241→
   242→### 5.1 HTTP AuthN/AuthZ (JWT/API key middleware)
   243→### 5.2 Secret Redaction (never persist secrets in Lago journal)
   244→### 5.3 Container Sandbox (bubblewrap or Apple Containers)
   245→### 5.4 Retention Policies (per-scope TTL + crypto-shredding)
   246→### 5.5 Network Egress Policy (deny-by-default for tool execution)
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Phase 6: Universal Data Plane & Platform (Weeks 17+)
   251→
   252→Future work, driven by need:
   253→- Lago Catalog (Assets + Representations, Unity Catalog-like hierarchy)
   254→- Lineage events (OpenLineage-compatible)
   255→- Semantic retrieval (vector index: LanceDB or Qdrant)
   256→- Graph memory (Mem0-style entity/relationship extraction)
   257→- DataFusion query layer (optional embedded analytics)
   258→- Delta/Iceberg representations (lakehouse interop)
   259→- Arrow Flight transport (high-throughput data streaming)
   260→- Multi-agent orchestration (fleet operation)
   261→
   262→---
   263→
   264→## Critical Path
   265→
   266→```
   267→Phase 0 (stabilize)
   268→    |
   269→Phase 1 (memory + context)  <-- biggest value unlock
   270→    |
   271→Phase 2 (self-learning + heartbeats)
   272→    |
   273→Phase 3 (skills + multi-provider)  <-- can parallel with Phase 2
   274→    |
   275→Phase 4 (observability)
   276→    |
   277→Phase 5 (security)
   278→    |
   279→Phase 6 (platform)
   280→```
   281→
   282→## Dependency Chain
   283→
   284→```
   285→lago-core events  -->  arcan-memory (new)  -->  context compiler  -->  self-learning
   286→                  \                                                  /
   287→                   -->  skill manifest  -->  skill loader  ----------
   288→                                                           \
   289→lago-policy       -->  memory governance  -->  promotion gates
   290→                  \
   291→                   -->  approval workflow  -->  heartbeat scheduler
   292→```
   293→
   294→## Testing Strategy
   295→
   296→| Phase | Test Type | Count Target | Validates |
   297→|-------|-----------|-------------|-----------|
   298→| 0 | Unit + Integration | 450+ | All crates compile, E2E round-trips |
   299→| 1 | Memory integration | 500+ | Memory persists, scopes enforced, context assembled |
   300→| 2 | Self-learning | 550+ | Proposals generated, gates enforced, heartbeats fire |
   301→| 3 | Multi-provider | 600+ | Provider fallback, skill loading, cost tracking |
   302→| 4 | Observability | 650+ | Traces correlate, replay matches, boundaries correct |
   303→| 5 | Security | 700+ | Auth rejects unauthorized, secrets scrubbed, container isolation |
   304→
   305→Test pyramid: ~70% unit, ~20% integration, ~10% E2E.
   306→
