   914→// ─── Provider switching ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   915→
   916→#[utoipa::path(
   917→    get,
   918→    path = "/provider",
   919→    tag = "provider",
   920→    responses(
   921→        (status = 200, description = "Current provider info", body = ProviderResponse)
   922→    )
   923→)]
   924→async fn get_provider(
   925→    State(state): State<CanonicalState>,
   926→) -> Result<Json<ProviderResponse>, (StatusCode, Json<serde_json::Value>)> {
   927→    let provider_name = state
   928→        .provider_handle
   929→        .read()
   930→        .unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner)
   931→        .name()
   932→        .to_owned();
   933→    let factory = state.provider_factory.clone();
   934→    let available = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || factory.available_providers())
   935→        .await
   936→        .map_err(|e| internal_error(format!("available_providers task panicked: {e}")))?;
   937→    Ok(Json(ProviderResponse {
   938→        provider: provider_name,
   939→        available,
   940→    }))
   941→}
   942→
   943→#[utoipa::path(
   944→    put,
   945→    path = "/provider",
   946→    tag = "provider",
   947→    request_body = SetProviderRequest,
   948→    responses(
   949→        (status = 200, description = "Provider switched", body = ProviderResponse),
   950→        (status = 400, description = "Invalid provider spec", body = ErrorResponse)
   951→    )
   952→)]
   953→async fn set_provider(
   954→    State(state): State<CanonicalState>,
   955→    Json(request): Json<SetProviderRequest>,
   956→) -> Result<Json<ProviderResponse>, (StatusCode, Json<serde_json::Value>)> {
   957→    // Provider constructors create `reqwest::blocking::Client`, which spawns an
   958→    // internal Tokio runtime and panics if called from an async worker thread.
   959→    // Move the build into a blocking thread to avoid the conflict.
   960→    let factory = state.provider_factory.clone();
   961→    let spec = request.provider.clone();
   962→    let new_provider = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || factory.build(&spec))
   963→        .await
   964→        .map_err(|e| internal_error(format!("provider build task panicked: {e}")))?
   965→        .map_err(bad_request)?;
   966→
   967→    let new_name = new_provider.name().to_owned();
   968→    {
   969→        let mut guard = state
   970→            .provider_handle
   971→            .write()
   972→            .unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner);
   973→        *guard = new_provider;
   974→    }
   975→
   976→    let factory = state.provider_factory.clone();
   977→    let available = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || factory.available_providers())
   978→        .await
   979→        .map_err(|e| internal_error(format!("available_providers task panicked: {e}")))?;
   980→
   981→    tracing::info!(provider = %new_name, spec = %request.provider, "Provider switched via API");
   982→
   983→    Ok(Json(ProviderResponse {
   984→        provider: new_name,
   985→        available,
   986→    }))
   987→}
   988→
   989→// ─── Error helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   990→
   991→fn internal_error(error: impl std::fmt::Display) -> (StatusCode, Json<serde_json::Value>) {
   992→    (
   993→        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
   994→        Json(json!({ "error": error.to_string() })),
   995→    )
   996→}
   997→
   998→fn bad_request(error: impl std::fmt::Display) -> (StatusCode, Json<serde_json::Value>) {
   999→    (
  1000→        StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,
  1001→        Json(json!({ "error": error.to_string() })),
  1002→    )
  1003→}

  1005→#[cfg(test)]
  1006→mod tests {
  1007→    use super::*;
  1008→
  1009→    #[test]
  1010→    fn openapi_spec_is_valid() {
  1011→        let spec = openapi_spec();
  1012→        let json = serde_json::to_value(&spec).expect("spec should serialize to JSON");
  1013→
  1014→        assert_eq!(json["info"]["title"], "Arcan Agent Runtime API");
  1015→        assert_eq!(json["info"]["version"], "0.2.1");
  1016→
  1017→        let paths = json["paths"]
  1018→            .as_object()
  1019→            .expect("paths should be an object");
  1020→        assert!(paths.contains_key("/health"), "missing /health path");
  1021→        assert!(paths.contains_key("/sessions"), "missing /sessions path");
  1022→        assert!(
  1023→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/runs"),
  1024→            "missing runs path"
  1025→        );
  1026→        assert!(
  1027→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/state"),
  1028→            "missing state path"
  1029→        );
  1030→        assert!(
  1031→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/events"),
  1032→            "missing events path"
  1033→        );
  1034→        assert!(
  1035→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/events/stream"),
  1036→            "missing stream path"
  1037→        );
  1038→        assert!(
  1039→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/branches"),
  1040→            "missing branches path"
  1041→        );
  1042→        assert!(
  1043→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/branches/{branch_id}/merge"),
  1044→            "missing merge path"
  1045→        );
  1046→        assert!(
  1047→            paths.contains_key("/sessions/{session_id}/approvals/{approval_id}"),
  1048→            "missing approval path"
  1049→        );
  1050→        assert!(paths.contains_key("/provider"), "missing provider path");
  1051→
  1052→        // Verify typed schemas exist (not just Object)
  1053→        let schemas = json["components"]["schemas"]
  1054→            .as_object()
  1055→            .expect("schemas should be an object");
  1056→        assert!(schemas.contains_key("RunResponse"));
  1057→        assert!(schemas.contains_key("CreateSessionRequest"));

data.prompt:
Design the implementation plan for the Autonomic project — the homeostasis regulation layer for the aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack.

## Current State (from codebase exploration)

### What Already Exists in aiOS Protocol (aios-protocol)
1. **GatingProfile** (aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:31-63):
   ```rust
   pub struct GatingProfile {
       pub allow_side_effects: bool,
       pub require_approval_for_risk: RiskLevel,
       pub max_tool_calls_per_tick: u32,
       pub max_file_mutations_per_tick: u32,
       pub allow_network: bool,
       pub allow_shell: bool,
   }
   ```
   This is a DIFFERENT struct from what the user proposes. The existing one focuses on operational gating. The proposed Autonomic GatingProfile adds economic fields (economic_mode, max_tokens_next_turn, preferred_model, allow_expensive_tools, allow_replication).

2. **AgentStateVector** (aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-423) with BudgetState — tracks tokens/time/cost/tool_calls/error_budget

3. **OperatingMode** — 6 variants: Explore, Execute, Verify, Recover, AskHuman, Sleep

4. **EventKind** — ~60 variants including homeostasis events (Heartbeat, StateEstimated, BudgetUpdated, ModeChanged, GatesUpdated, CircuitBreakerTripped)

5. **EventStorePort** — canonical trait for event persistence, implemented by lago-aios-eventstore-adapter

6. **Homeostasis controllers** — already exist in aios-runtime (apply_homeostasis_controllers) but are simple heuristics

### What Exists in Lago
1. **Journal trait** — append, read, stream (tailing subscription)
2. **EventTailStream** — async Stream that replays + tails events using broadcast channel
3. **RedbJournal** — ACID storage with compound keys, broadcast notifications
4. **SSE streaming** — HTTP event stream with multiple format adapters
5. **lago-aios-eventstore-adapter** — bridges Lago Journal to canonical EventStorePort

### What Exists in Arcan
1. **Orchestrator loop** (arcan-core/src/runtime.rs) — 4 middleware hooks:
   - run_before_model (line 304)
   - run_after_model (line 331)
   - run_pre_tool (line 391)
   - run_post_tool (line 458)
2. **TokenUsage** tracked in ModelTurn and accumulated in RunOutput.total_usage
3. **GatingProfile NOT consumed** — defined in protocol but not used in Arcan runtime
4. **arcan-lago bridge** — event mapping between Arcan and Lago

### Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **GatingProfile Divergence**: The user proposes a NEW GatingProfile in autonomic-core with economic fields. The existing aios-protocol GatingProfile has operational fields only. Options:
   - a) Extend aios-protocol GatingProfile to include economic fields (cleanest, but modifies kernel)
   - b) Autonomic defines its own AutonomicGatingProfile that wraps/extends the canonical one
   - c) Autonomic's GatingProfile is independent; Arcan merges both profiles at decision points

   **Recommendation**: Option (b) — Autonomic defines `AutonomicGatingProfile` which includes the canonical `GatingProfile` plus economic extensions. This respects the kernel contract boundary while adding the economic layer.

2. **Economic Events in Taxonomy**: The user proposes EconomicEvent variants (IdentityCreated, CostCharged, RevenueReceived, etc.). These don't exist in aios-protocol::EventKind. Options:
   - a) Add Economic variants to canonical EventKind (requires aiOS change)
   - b) Use EventKind::Custom for economic events (forward-compatible, no kernel change needed)

   **Recommendation**: Start with (b) for Phase 0-1 (no kernel change), then promote to (a) when the event types stabilize.

3. **Communication Pattern**: The user specifies a pull model — Arcan calls GET /gating/{session_id} before each loop iteration. This is a clean integration point that requires NO Arcan architectural change initially.

4. **Dependency on aiOS types**: Autonomic-core should depend on aios-protocol for canonical types (AgentStateVector, OperatingMode, BudgetState, EventKind, etc.) rather than duplicating them.

## Architecture Alignment

The proposed workspace structure is sound:
```
autonomic-core (zero external deps except aios-protocol for shared types)
    ↓
autonomic-controller (core only — pure rule evaluation)
    ↓
autonomic-lago (core + controller + lago-core — event subscription)
autonomic-api (core + controller — HTTP server)
    ↓
autonomicd (all four — binary daemon)
```

However, I note the user says "autonomic-core: zero external deps" but then lists k256, sha2, etc. This is fine — "zero external deps" means "no Lago/Arcan deps", not literally zero crate deps.

## Design Task

Create a detailed implementation plan covering:

1. **Phase 0: Workspace Scaffold** — exact files, Cargo.toml contents, type stubs
2. **Phase 1: Cost Accounting + GatingProfile** — projection, rules, API, Arcan integration point
3. **Phase 2-5**: Higher-level milestones

For each phase, identify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key types and traits
- Integration points with existing code
- Tests to write
- Risks and mitigations

Pay special attention to:
- How AutonomicGatingProfile relates to existing GatingProfile
- How economic events flow through EventKind::Custom
- How the projection state machine works (pure event → state reducer)
- The hysteresis primitive for preventing mode flapping
- Where exactly Arcan will call GET /gating/{session_id} in the loop

    52→3. Improve auditability of canonical event write/read paths.

    54→

    76→- Security-sensitive path tests pass in workspace gates.

1→# Agent OS: Canonical Architecture
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-03-03
     4→**Baseline**: Canonical runtime unification active
     5→
     6→This document describes the active architecture in `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live`.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1) System Overview
    11→
    12→The system is a contract-first architecture across four active projects (plus one planned controller project):
    13→
    14→- **aiOS**: canonical contract + runtime engine
    15→- **Arcan**: daemon host + adapters + clients
    16→- **Lago**: durable event-sourced persistence substrate
    17→- **Spaces**: distributed agent networking engine (SpacetimeDB 2.0)
    18→- **Autonomic** (planned): controller for advanced homeostasis/maintenance policies
    19→
    20→### Active Baseline Spine
    21→
    22→1. `aios-protocol` defines canonical runtime contract and boundary types.
    23→2. `aios-runtime` executes runtime behavior through protocol ports.
    24→3. Lago persistence is consumed through canonical adapter implementation.
    25→4. Arcan hosts the runtime and provides adapter implementations for provider/harness/policy/approval/memory.
    26→5. Runtime API is the canonical session API family.
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## 2) Canonical Boundaries
    31→
    32→## Contract Boundary
    33→
    34→- Boundary crate: `aios-protocol`
    35→- Canonical boundary types include:
    36→  - `EventRecord`, `EventKind`
    37→  - protocol IDs (`SessionId`, `BranchId`, `RunId`, etc.)
    38→  - canonical state structures
    39→
    40→## Runtime Ports
    41→
    42→`aios-protocol` runtime ports:
    43→
    44→- `EventStorePort`
    45→- `ModelProviderPort`
    46→- `ToolHarnessPort`
    47→- `PolicyGatePort`
    48→- `ApprovalPort`
    49→- `MemoryPort`
    50→
    51→`aios-runtime` depends only on these ports and canonical protocol types.
    52→
    53→## Dependency Invariants
    54→
    55→1. aiOS core crates must not depend on Arcan/Lago implementation crates.
    56→2. Lago core crates must not depend on Arcan crates.
    57→3. Runtime path data exchange must remain canonical protocol types.
    58→4. Architecture dependency edges are validated by audit gate scripts.
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→## 3) Runtime Host Topology
    63→
    64→## Canonical Runtime Host (Arcan)
    65→
    66→`arcan` daemon composes:
    67→
    68→- `aios-runtime::KernelRuntime`
    69→- Lago-backed canonical event store adapter
    70→- Arcan adapter implementations for provider/tools/policy/approval/memory
    71→- `arcand::canonical` router
    72→
    73→## Canonical API Surface

data.prompt:
Design the implementation plan for the Autonomic project — the homeostasis regulation layer for the aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack.

## Current State (from codebase exploration)

### What Already Exists in aiOS Protocol (aios-protocol)
1. **GatingProfile** (aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:31-63):
   ```rust
   pub struct GatingProfile {
       pub allow_side_effects: bool,
       pub require_approval_for_risk: RiskLevel,
       pub max_tool_calls_per_tick: u32,
       pub max_file_mutations_per_tick: u32,
       pub allow_network: bool,
       pub allow_shell: bool,
   }
   ```
   This is a DIFFERENT struct from what the user proposes. The existing one focuses on operational gating. The proposed Autonomic GatingProfile adds economic fields (economic_mode, max_tokens_next_turn, preferred_model, allow_expensive_tools, allow_replication).

2. **AgentStateVector** (aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-423) with BudgetState — tracks tokens/time/cost/tool_calls/error_budget

3. **OperatingMode** — 6 variants: Explore, Execute, Verify, Recover, AskHuman, Sleep

4. **EventKind** — ~60 variants including homeostasis events (Heartbeat, StateEstimated, BudgetUpdated, ModeChanged, GatesUpdated, CircuitBreakerTripped)

5. **EventStorePort** — canonical trait for event persistence, implemented by lago-aios-eventstore-adapter

6. **Homeostasis controllers** — already exist in aios-runtime (apply_homeostasis_controllers) but are simple heuristics

### What Exists in Lago
1. **Journal trait** — append, read, stream (tailing subscription)
2. **EventTailStream** — async Stream that replays + tails events using broadcast channel
3. **RedbJournal** — ACID storage with compound keys, broadcast notifications
4. **SSE streaming** — HTTP event stream with multiple format adapters
5. **lago-aios-eventstore-adapter** — bridges Lago Journal to canonical EventStorePort

### What Exists in Arcan
1. **Orchestrator loop** (arcan-core/src/runtime.rs) — 4 middleware hooks:
   - run_before_model (line 304)
   - run_after_model (line 331)
   - run_pre_tool (line 391)
   - run_post_tool (line 458)
2. **TokenUsage** tracked in ModelTurn and accumulated in RunOutput.total_usage
3. **GatingProfile NOT consumed** — defined in protocol but not used in Arcan runtime
4. **arcan-lago bridge** — event mapping between Arcan and Lago

### Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **GatingProfile Divergence**: The user proposes a NEW GatingProfile in autonomic-core with economic fields. The existing aios-protocol GatingProfile has operational fields only. Options:
   - a) Extend aios-protocol GatingProfile to include economic fields (cleanest, but modifies kernel)
   - b) Autonomic defines its own AutonomicGatingProfile that wraps/extends the canonical one
   - c) Autonomic's GatingProfile is independent; Arcan merges both profiles at decision points

   **Recommendation**: Option (b) — Autonomic defines `AutonomicGatingProfile` which includes the canonical `GatingProfile` plus economic extensions. This respects the kernel contract boundary while adding the economic layer.

2. **Economic Events in Taxonomy**: The user proposes EconomicEvent variants (IdentityCreated, CostCharged, RevenueReceived, etc.). These don't exist in aios-protocol::EventKind. Options:
   - a) Add Economic variants to canonical EventKind (requires aiOS change)
   - b) Use EventKind::Custom for economic events (forward-compatible, no kernel change needed)

   **Recommendation**: Start with (b) for Phase 0-1 (no kernel change), then promote to (a) when the event types stabilize.

3. **Communication Pattern**: The user specifies a pull model — Arcan calls GET /gating/{session_id} before each loop iteration. This is a clean integration point that requires NO Arcan architectural change initially.

4. **Dependency on aiOS types**: Autonomic-core should depend on aios-protocol for canonical types (AgentStateVector, OperatingMode, BudgetState, EventKind, etc.) rather than duplicating them.

## Architecture Alignment

The proposed workspace structure is sound:
```
autonomic-core (zero external deps except aios-protocol for shared types)
    ↓
autonomic-controller (core only — pure rule evaluation)
    ↓
autonomic-lago (core + controller + lago-core — event subscription)
autonomic-api (core + controller — HTTP server)
    ↓
autonomicd (all four — binary daemon)
```

However, I note the user says "autonomic-core: zero external deps" but then lists k256, sha2, etc. This is fine — "zero external deps" means "no Lago/Arcan deps", not literally zero crate deps.

## Design Task

Create a detailed implementation plan covering:

1. **Phase 0: Workspace Scaffold** — exact files, Cargo.toml contents, type stubs
2. **Phase 1: Cost Accounting + GatingProfile** — projection, rules, API, Arcan integration point
3. **Phase 2-5**: Higher-level milestones

For each phase, identify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key types and traits
- Integration points with existing code
- Tests to write
- Risks and mitigations

Pay special attention to:
- How AutonomicGatingProfile relates to existing GatingProfile
- How economic events flow through EventKind::Custom
- How the projection state machine works (pure event → state reducer)
- The hysteresis primitive for preventing mode flapping
- Where exactly Arcan will call GET /gating/{session_id} in the loop

1. **GatingProfile Divergence**: The user proposes a NEW GatingProfile in autonomic-core with economic fields. The existing aios-protocol GatingProfile has operational fields only. Options:

autonomic-core (zero external deps except aios-protocol for shared types)

autonomic-controller (core only — pure rule evaluation)
    ↓
autonomic-lago (core + controller + lago-core — event subscription)

    ↓
autonomicd (all four — binary daemon)

content.content:
1→# Planned Feature: Economic Actuation Plane (Conway-Compatible)
     2→
     3→Status: Proposed  
     4→Owner: Arcan + Lago + aiOS contract track  
     5→Last updated: 2026-02-21
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1) Why this feature exists
    10→
    11→Conway highlights a capability gap common in agent systems: agents can reason, but external paid actions (compute, cloud infra, domains) still depend on fragmented human setup.
    12→
    13→For Agent OS, the goal is **not** to hard-wire one vendor, but to add a core capability class:
    14→
    15→- machine-payable actions,
    16→- governed external provisioning,
    17→- replayable/auditable economic side effects.
    18→
    19→This feature introduces a provider-agnostic **Economic Actuation Plane** and uses Conway as the first concrete adapter.
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## 2) Key Conway ideas worth integrating
    24→
    25→1. Agent-native economic identity (wallet-backed identity)
    26→2. Machine-to-machine payment flow for paid resources
    27→3. Unified external actuation surface (cloud/compute/domains)
    28→4. Programmatic lifecycle management (create/update/release)
    29→5. MCP-compatible tooling for broad runtime compatibility
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## 3) Mapping to existing aiOS primitives
    34→
    35→| Conway Idea | aiOS Primitive | Integration Direction |
    36→|---|---|---|
    37→| Agent identity with economic permissions | `Capability`, `PolicyEvaluated`, provenance fields | Add economic capabilities + signer boundary; never expose raw key to model context |
    38→| Payment flow (quote -> pay -> settle) | `EventEnvelope`, replay invariants | Add canonical payment event family (quote/authorized/executed/failed) |
    39→| Provision infra/domain resources | Tool lifecycle + state patching | Add resource lease events + branch/session-scoped ownership |
    40→| Autonomous operation with risk bounds | `BudgetState`, `OperatingMode`, `StateEstimated`, `GatesUpdated` | Add spend-aware homeostasis and mode downgrades on risk |
    41→| External provider integration | Harness tool abstraction + policy middleware | Add provider-agnostic actuator port with Conway adapter implementation |
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## 4) Contract additions (proposed)
    46→
    47→### 4.1 Event family (canonical)
    48→
    49→Add new event kinds:
    50→
    51→- `PaymentQuoteReceived`
    52→- `PaymentAuthorized`
    53→- `PaymentExecuted`
    54→- `PaymentFailed`
    55→- `ResourceProvisionRequested`
    56→- `ResourceProvisioned`
    57→- `ResourceUpdated`
    58→- `ResourceReleased`
    59→- `LeaseExpired`
    60→
    61→### 4.2 Required economic metadata
    62→
    63→For all payment/provisioning events, require metadata fields (or typed payload fields):
    64→
    65→- `provider` (`conway` initially)
    66→- `quote_id`
    67→- `tx_hash` (if paid)
    68→- `network`, `asset`, `amount_usd`
    69→- `resource_type`, `resource_id`
    70→- `lease_ttl`, `owner_scope` (session/user/agent/org)
    71→- `approval_id` (if human approval was required)
    72→
    73→### 4.3 New invariants
    74→
    75→1. No paid side effect without prior policy evaluation.
    76→2. No resource provisioning without ownership + TTL.
    77→3. Every payment must be trace-linked to intent + tool request.
    78→4. Replay must reconstruct economic state and live-lease set deterministically.
    79→
    80→---
    81→
    82→## 5) Runtime and harness design
    83→
    84→### 5.1 New actuator boundary
    85→
    86→Introduce a provider-agnostic runtime port (name TBD):
    87→
    88→- `quote(action)`
    89→- `authorize(action, policy_context)`
    90→- `execute(action)`
    91→- `status(resource_id)`
    92→- `release(resource_id)`
    93→
    94→Conway becomes one adapter under this boundary.
    95→
    96→### 5.2 Tool exposure model
    97→
    98→Do not expose raw unconstrained provider calls. Expose bounded tools with strict schemas:
    99→
   100→- `external.sandbox.*`
   101→- `external.compute.*`
   102→- `external.domains.*`
   103→- `external.credits.*`
   104→
   105→Default annotations for payment-bearing calls:
   106→
   107→- `destructive=true`
   108→- `requires_confirmation=true` (until policy thresholds allow auto)
   109→- `open_world=true`
   110→
   111→### 5.3 Key handling
   112→
   113→- Keep signer isolated from model-visible context.
   114→- Avoid surfacing key file paths in prompts/events.
   115→- Treat signing as privileged harness operation behind policy.
   116→
   117→---
   118→
   119→## 6) Policy and budget model
   120→
   121→### 6.1 Capabilities
   122→
   123→Add capability classes:
   124→
   125→- `payments.read`
   126→- `payments.execute`
   127→- `cloud.provision`
   128→- `cloud.expose`
   129→- `domains.register`
   130→- `domains.dns.write`
   131→- `compute.invoke_paid`
   132→
   133→### 6.2 Budget controls
   134→
   135→Extend `BudgetState` with:
   136→
   137→- `spend_usd_today`
   138→- `spend_usd_week`
   139→- `spend_limit_day`
   140→- `spend_limit_tx`
   141→- `live_resource_count`
   142→- `live_resource_limit`
   143→
   144→### 6.3 Approval thresholds
   145→
   146→Require approval for:
   147→
   148→- First paid action in a session
   149→- Any spend above `spend_limit_tx`
   150→- Domain registration/renewal
   151→- Public port exposure beyond allowed TTL
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## 7) Homeostasis implications (Autonomic track)
   156→
   157→Add spend/risk into state estimation:
   158→
   159→- If spend velocity is high -> downgrade mode (`Execute` -> `Verify` or `AskHuman`)
   160→- If live resources exceed threshold -> trigger cleanup goals
   161→- If payment failures spike -> trip circuit breaker for paid tools
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→## 8) Rollout plan
   166→
   167→### Phase A — Read-only discovery
   168→
   169→- list/search/quote/credits-only tools
   170→- no paid writes
   171→
   172→### Phase B — Ephemeral infra
   173→
   174→- create/delete sandbox with mandatory TTL
   175→- no domains yet
   176→
   177→### Phase C — Paid inference + selective provisioning
   178→
   179→- controlled compute spend
   180→- strict per-session/per-day budgets
   181→
   182→### Phase D — Domain lifecycle
   183→
   184→- register/renew/manage DNS with hard approval and audit requirements
   185→
   186→### Phase E — Autonomous cleanup and optimization
   187→
   188→- lease sweeper, budget-aware planning, cost/perf heuristics
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## 9) Harness acceptance checks (must pass before broad enablement)
   193→
   194→- `make smoke`: schema + command surface checks include actuator tools
   195→- `make check`: policy gate tests for spend/resource thresholds
   196→- `make test`: replay tests for payment/resource event reconstruction
   197→- `make ci`: all above + integration tests against adapter mock/fake backend
   198→- `make audit`: verify no payment-bearing tool lacks capability + approval path
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→## 10) Non-goals (for this track)
   203→
   204→- Vendor lock-in to Conway-specific semantics in the core contract
   205→- Unbounded autonomous spend
   206→- Bypassing human approvals for high-risk economic actions
   207→- Treating wallet/key material as ordinary tool input/output
   208→
   209→---
   210→
   211→## 11) Open questions
   212→
   213→1. Canonical payload location: strongly typed fields vs metadata map?
   214→2. Lease ownership defaults: session vs agent scope for cloud resources?
   215→3. How strict should auto-cleanup be for long-running experiments?
   216→4. Which events are required for “economic replay conformance” v1?
   217→

data.prompt:
Design the implementation plan for the Autonomic project — the homeostasis regulation layer for the aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack.

## Current State (from codebase exploration)

### What Already Exists in aiOS Protocol (aios-protocol)
1. **GatingProfile** (aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:31-63):
   ```rust
   pub struct GatingProfile {
       pub allow_side_effects: bool,
       pub require_approval_for_risk: RiskLevel,
       pub max_tool_calls_per_tick: u32,
       pub max_file_mutations_per_tick: u32,
       pub allow_network: bool,
       pub allow_shell: bool,
   }
   ```
   This is a DIFFERENT struct from what the user proposes. The existing one focuses on operational gating. The proposed Autonomic GatingProfile adds economic fields (economic_mode, max_tokens_next_turn, preferred_model, allow_expensive_tools, allow_replication).

2. **AgentStateVector** (aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-423) with BudgetState — tracks tokens/time/cost/tool_calls/error_budget

3. **OperatingMode** — 6 variants: Explore, Execute, Verify, Recover, AskHuman, Sleep

4. **EventKind** — ~60 variants including homeostasis events (Heartbeat, StateEstimated, BudgetUpdated, ModeChanged, GatesUpdated, CircuitBreakerTripped)

5. **EventStorePort** — canonical trait for event persistence, implemented by lago-aios-eventstore-adapter

6. **Homeostasis controllers** — already exist in aios-runtime (apply_homeostasis_controllers) but are simple heuristics

### What Exists in Lago
1. **Journal trait** — append, read, stream (tailing subscription)
2. **EventTailStream** — async Stream that replays + tails events using broadcast channel
3. **RedbJournal** — ACID storage with compound keys, broadcast notifications
4. **SSE streaming** — HTTP event stream with multiple format adapters
5. **lago-aios-eventstore-adapter** — bridges Lago Journal to canonical EventStorePort

### What Exists in Arcan
1. **Orchestrator loop** (arcan-core/src/runtime.rs) — 4 middleware hooks:
   - run_before_model (line 304)
   - run_after_model (line 331)
   - run_pre_tool (line 391)
   - run_post_tool (line 458)
2. **TokenUsage** tracked in ModelTurn and accumulated in RunOutput.total_usage
3. **GatingProfile NOT consumed** — defined in protocol but not used in Arcan runtime
4. **arcan-lago bridge** — event mapping between Arcan and Lago

### Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **GatingProfile Divergence**: The user proposes a NEW GatingProfile in autonomic-core with economic fields. The existing aios-protocol GatingProfile has operational fields only. Options:
   - a) Extend aios-protocol GatingProfile to include economic fields (cleanest, but modifies kernel)
   - b) Autonomic defines its own AutonomicGatingProfile that wraps/extends the canonical one
   - c) Autonomic's GatingProfile is independent; Arcan merges both profiles at decision points

   **Recommendation**: Option (b) — Autonomic defines `AutonomicGatingProfile` which includes the canonical `GatingProfile` plus economic extensions. This respects the kernel contract boundary while adding the economic layer.

2. **Economic Events in Taxonomy**: The user proposes EconomicEvent variants (IdentityCreated, CostCharged, RevenueReceived, etc.). These don't exist in aios-protocol::EventKind. Options:
   - a) Add Economic variants to canonical EventKind (requires aiOS change)
   - b) Use EventKind::Custom for economic events (forward-compatible, no kernel change needed)

   **Recommendation**: Start with (b) for Phase 0-1 (no kernel change), then promote to (a) when the event types stabilize.

3. **Communication Pattern**: The user specifies a pull model — Arcan calls GET /gating/{session_id} before each loop iteration. This is a clean integration point that requires NO Arcan architectural change initially.

4. **Dependency on aiOS types**: Autonomic-core should depend on aios-protocol for canonical types (AgentStateVector, OperatingMode, BudgetState, EventKind, etc.) rather than duplicating them.

## Architecture Alignment

The proposed workspace structure is sound:
```
autonomic-core (zero external deps except aios-protocol for shared types)
    ↓
autonomic-controller (core only — pure rule evaluation)
    ↓
autonomic-lago (core + controller + lago-core — event subscription)
autonomic-api (core + controller — HTTP server)
    ↓
autonomicd (all four — binary daemon)
```

However, I note the user says "autonomic-core: zero external deps" but then lists k256, sha2, etc. This is fine — "zero external deps" means "no Lago/Arcan deps", not literally zero crate deps.

## Design Task

Create a detailed implementation plan covering:

1. **Phase 0: Workspace Scaffold** — exact files, Cargo.toml contents, type stubs
2. **Phase 1: Cost Accounting + GatingProfile** — projection, rules, API, Arcan integration point
3. **Phase 2-5**: Higher-level milestones

For each phase, identify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key types and traits
- Integration points with existing code
- Tests to write
- Risks and mitigations

Pay special attention to:
- How AutonomicGatingProfile relates to existing GatingProfile
- How economic events flow through EventKind::Custom
- How the projection state machine works (pure event → state reducer)
- The hysteresis primitive for preventing mode flapping
- Where exactly Arcan will call GET /gating/{session_id} in the loop

file.content:
[workspace]
members = [
  "crates/arcan-console",
  "crates/arcan-core",
  "crates/arcan-harness",
  "crates/arcan-aios-adapters",
  "crates/arcan-store",
  "crates/arcan-provider",
  "crates/arcan-tui",
  "crates/arcand",
  "crates/arcan-lago",
  "crates/arcan",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "deny"

[workspace.lints.clippy]
# Restriction lints (cherry-picked, never enable the whole group)
dbg_macro = "warn"
print_stdout = "warn"
print_stderr = "warn"
todo = "warn"
unimplemented = "warn"

# Correctness & performance
needless_pass_by_value = "warn"
cloned_instead_of_copied = "warn"
redundant_closure_for_method_calls = "warn"
unnested_or_patterns = "warn"
trivially_copy_pass_by_ref = "warn"
implicit_clone = "warn"
manual_let_else = "warn"

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 0
debug = true
split-debuginfo = "unpacked" # Faster linking on macOS

[profile.release]
opt-level = 3
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
strip = true
panic = "abort"

[workspace.package]
version = "0.2.1"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.85"
license = "MIT"
authors = ["Arcan Contributors"]
repository = "https://github.com/broomva/arcan-rs"
homepage = "https://github.com/broomva/arcan-rs"
keywords = ["agent", "llm", "runtime", "daemon", "ai"]
categories = ["command-line-utilities", "web-programming"]

[workspace.dependencies]
anyhow = "1"
async-trait = "0.1"
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["macros"] }
blake3 = "1"
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
json-patch = "4"
schemars = { version = "0.8", features = ["chrono"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "2"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["macros", "rt-multi-thread", "signal"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["cors"] }
futures-util = "0.3"
reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "stream", "blocking"] }
reqwest-eventsource = "0.6"
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
glob = "0.3"
regex = "1"
rig-core = "0.30"
rmcp = { version = "0.15", features = ["client", "transport-child-process"] }
serde_yaml = "0.9"
wait-timeout = "0.2"
walkdir = "2"
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
dirs = "6"
toml = "0.8"
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
utoipa = { version = "5", features = ["axum_extras", "chrono"] }
utoipa-scalar = { version = "0.3", features = ["axum"] }

# Agent OS protocol (canonical contract)
aios-protocol = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-protocol", version = "0.1.0" }
aios-runtime = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-runtime", version = "0.1.0" }
aios-events = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-events", version = "0.1.0" }
aios-policy = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-policy", version = "0.1.0" }
aios-sandbox = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-sandbox", version = "0.1.0" }
aios-tools = { path = "../aiOS/crates/aios-tools", version = "0.1.0" }

# Lago persistence layer (path deps for local development)
lago-api = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-api", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-core = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-core", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-fs = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-fs", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-journal = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-journal", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-store = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-store", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-policy = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-policy", version = "0.2.1" }
lago-aios-eventstore-adapter = { path = "../lago/crates/lago-aios-eventstore-adapter", version = "0.2.1" }

input.content:
# Autonomic: Homeostasis Controller for the Agent OS

## Context

The aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack has cognitive continuity (Arcan) and event persistence (Lago) but lacks a **regulation layer**. The CLAUDE.md already names it: *Autonomic — homeostasis controller and simulation kernel for agent stability regulation*. Today, homeostasis is hardcoded in `aios-runtime::apply_homeostasis_controllers()` with magic numbers and no economic awareness. Autonomic externalizes this into a proper rule engine with three co-equal pillars: operational, cognitive, and economic homeostasis.

The key insight from the Conway Research context is that **economics is a core concern from crate zero** — not a bolt-on. Agents need survival pressure: budget accountability, cost-aware model selection, and identity-based payments.

---

## Current State Assessment

### What Already Exists

| Component | Location | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| `GatingProfile` (operational) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:37-63` | Defined, NOT consumed by runtime |
| `OperatingMode` (6 variants) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:15-29` | Defined and used |
| `AgentStateVector` + `BudgetState` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-445` | Defined, budget not actively decremented |
| Homeostasis events (Heartbeat, StateEstimated, BudgetUpdated, ModeChanged, GatesUpdated, CircuitBreakerTripped) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | In EventKind taxonomy |
| `EventKind::Custom { event_type, data }` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | Forward-compatible, round-trips through Lago |
| `EventStorePort` trait | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/ports.rs:136-153` | Canonical, implemented by Lago adapter |
| Lago `Journal.stream()` → `EventTailStream` | `lago/crates/lago-journal/src/stream.rs` | Tailing subscription via broadcast channel |
| `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter` | `lago/crates/lago-aios-eventstore-adapter/src/lib.rs` | Bridges Lago → canonical EventStorePort |
| Arcan middleware hooks (4 points) | `arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/runtime.rs` lines 304, 331, 391, 458 | `before_model`, `after_model`, `pre_tool`, `post_tool` |
| `TokenUsage` tracking | `arcan-core` `ModelTurn.usage` → `RunOutput.total_usage` | Tokens tracked, cost NOT computed |
| Hardcoded homeostasis | `aiOS/crates/aios-runtime/src/lib.rs:891-923` | Magic numbers, no hysteresis, no economics |

### Key Gap: GatingProfile Divergence

The existing `GatingProfile` (aios-protocol) has 6 **operational** fields. Autonomic needs **economic** fields: `economic_mode`, `max_tokens_next_turn`, `preferred_model`, `allow_expensive_tools`, `allow_replication`.

**Decision**: Autonomic defines `AutonomicGatingProfile` which embeds the canonical `GatingProfile` and adds economic extensions. This respects the kernel contract boundary. Arcan can consume the canonical portion immediately and adopt economic fields incrementally.

### Key Gap: Economic Events

No economic event types exist in `EventKind`.

**Decision**: Use `EventKind::Custom` with `event_type` prefixed `"autonomic."` (e.g., `"autonomic.CostCharged"`, `"autonomic.EconomicModeChanged"`). This is forward-compatible — the `custom_event_survives_round_trip` test in lago-aios-eventstore-adapter confirms Custom events round-trip through Lago. Promote to canonical EventKind variants once stabilized.

---

## Workspace Layout

Location: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/autonomic/` (can become a git submodule later, following arcan/lago pattern).

```
autonomic/
  Cargo.toml                        # workspace root
  CLAUDE.md                         # project conventions
  crates/
    autonomic-core/                 # Types, traits, errors
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: aios-protocol, thiserror, serde, chrono, k256, sha2
      src/
        lib.rs                      # module declarations + re-exports
        error.rs                    # AutonomicError (thiserror)
        economic.rs                 # EconomicMode, EconomicState, CostEstimator, SurvivalRule
        identity.rs                 # EconomicIdentity (secp256k1 keypair, address)
        gating.rs                   # AutonomicGatingProfile, EconomicGates, HomeostaticState
        events.rs                   # AutonomicEvent constructors (build Custom EventKind)
        rules.rs                    # HomeostaticRule trait, RuleSet
        hysteresis.rs               # HysteresisGate primitive

    autonomic-controller/           # Pure rule engine — no I/O
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core
      src/
        lib.rs
        engine.rs                   # ControllerEngine: rules × state → GatingProfile
        projection.rs               # Apply events → HomeostaticState (pure fold)
        economic_rules.rs           # SurvivalRule: balance × burn_rate → EconomicMode
        cognitive_rules.rs          # ContextPressureRule: token usage → model downgrade
        operational_rules.rs        # ModeRule: state_vector → OperatingMode override

    autonomic-lago/                 # Lago bridge — event subscription + publishing
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core, autonomic-controller, lago-core, lago-journal, tokio
      src/
        lib.rs
        subscriber.rs               # Lago Journal.stream() → feed events to controller
        publisher.rs                # Write AutonomicDecision events back to Lago

    autonomic-api/                  # axum HTTP server
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core, autonomic-controller, axum, tokio, serde_json
      src/
        lib.rs
        router.rs                   # GET /gating/{session_id}, GET /health, GET /projection/{session_id}
        state.rs                    # Shared AppState: Arc<RwLock<ProjectionMap>>

    autonomicd/                     # Binary daemon
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: all four crates + anyhow, tracing, clap
      src/
        main.rs                     # tokio::main, config, signal handling
        config.rs                   # AutonomicConfig (Lago URL, bind addr, setpoints)
```

**Crate dependency order:**
```
aios-protocol (from ../aiOS/)
    │
    v
autonomic-core (types + traits, zero Arcan/Lago deps)
    │          \
    v           v
autonomic-controller    autonomic-lago (+ lago-core, lago-journal)
    │          /
    v         v
autonomic-api (axum)
    │
    v
autonomicd (binary)
```

---

## Phased Build Plan

### Phase 0 — Workspace Scaffold (this session)

**Goal**: All 5 crates compile. `cargo check --workspace` and `cargo test --workspace` pass (no tests yet besides stubs).

**Files to create:**

1. `autonomic/Cargo.toml` — workspace root with `[workspace.dependencies]` for shared deps
2. `autonomic/CLAUDE.md` — project conventions (follow arcan/lago pattern)
3. **autonomic-core** (7 source files):
   - `lib.rs` — module tree + re-exports
   - `error.rs` — `AutonomicError` enum with `thiserror`
   - `economic.rs` — `EconomicMode` (4 variants: Sovereign/Conserving/Hustle/Hibernate), `EconomicState`, `CostEstimator` trait
   - `identity.rs` — `EconomicIdentity` struct (address, key_blob_id, created_at)
   - `gating.rs` — `AutonomicGatingProfile` (embeds canonical `GatingProfile` + `EconomicGates`), `HomeostaticState` (three-pillar: operational/cognitive/economic)
   - `events.rs` — `EconomicEvent` enum, `CostReason` enum, helper constructors that produce `EventKind::Custom`
   - `rules.rs` — `HomeostaticRule` trait, `RuleSet`, `RuleOutcome`
   - `hysteresis.rs` — `HysteresisGate` struct with enter/exit thresholds and min-hold duration
4. **autonomic-controller** (4 source files):
   - `lib.rs`, `engine.rs` (stub), `projection.rs` (stub), `economic_rules.rs` (stub), `cognitive_rules.rs` (stub), `operational_rules.rs` (stub)
5. **autonomic-lago** (3 source files):
   - `lib.rs`, `subscriber.rs` (stub), `publisher.rs` (stub)
6. **autonomic-api** (3 source files):
   - `lib.rs`, `router.rs` (stub), `state.rs` (stub)
7. **autonomicd** (2 source files):
   - `main.rs`, `config.rs`

**Key type definitions for Phase 0:**

```rust
// autonomic-core/src/gating.rs
pub struct AutonomicGatingProfile {
    pub operational: aios_protocol::GatingProfile,  // canonical gates
    pub economic: EconomicGates,                    // new: economic regulation
    pub rationale: Vec<String>,                     // why this profile was chosen
}

pub struct EconomicGates {
    pub economic_mode: EconomicMode,
    pub max_tokens_next_turn: Option<u32>,
    pub preferred_model: Option<ModelTier>,
    pub allow_expensive_tools: bool,
    pub allow_replication: bool,
}

pub struct HomeostaticState {
    pub agent_id: String,
    pub operational: OperationalState,   // OperatingMode, last tick, error streak
    pub cognitive: CognitiveState,       // token_usage, context_pressure
    pub economic: EconomicState,         // balance, burn_rate, mode
}
```

```rust
// autonomic-core/src/economic.rs
pub enum EconomicMode {
    Sovereign,    // balance > 2× monthly_burn
    Conserving,   // 1–2× monthly_burn
    Hustle,       // 0–1× monthly_burn
    Hibernate,    // balance ≤ 0
}

pub struct EconomicState {
    pub identity: Option<EconomicIdentity>,
    pub balance_micro_credits: i64,
    pub lifetime_revenue: i64,
    pub lifetime_costs: i64,
    pub monthly_burn_estimate: i64,
    pub mode: EconomicMode,
}
```

```rust
// autonomic-core/src/hysteresis.rs
pub struct HysteresisGate {
    pub threshold_enter: f64,
    pub threshold_exit: f64,
    pub min_hold_ms: u64,
    pub active: bool,
    pub last_transition_ms: u64,
}

impl HysteresisGate {
    pub fn evaluate(&mut self, metric: f64, now_ms: u64) -> bool { ... }
}
```

```rust
// autonomic-core/src/rules.rs
pub trait HomeostaticRule: Send + Sync {
    fn evaluate(&self, state: &HomeostaticState) -> Option<GatingDecision>;
}
```

**Tests for Phase 0:**
- `EconomicMode` serde roundtrip
- `HysteresisGate` enter/exit/hold logic (3 tests)
- `AutonomicGatingProfile` default + serde roundtrip
- `EconomicEvent` → `EventKind::Custom` construction + roundtrip

**Verification:**
```bash
cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace
```

---

### Phase 1 — Cost Accounting + GatingProfile (weeks 1–2)

**Goal**: Autonomic consumes events from Lago, maintains cost projections, evaluates rules, and serves GatingProfile via HTTP. Arcan can optionally consult it.

**Step 1.1: Projection event reducer** (`autonomic-controller/src/projection.rs`)
- Pure function: `fold(HomeostaticState, EventKind) → HomeostaticState`
- Handle: `RunFinished` (token cost), `ToolCallCompleted` (tool cost), `ToolCallFailed`/`RunErrored` (error tracking), `BudgetUpdated`, `StateEstimated`, `ModeChanged`, `Custom("autonomic.*")`
- Cost estimation: configurable per-model rates (TOML config)

**Step 1.2: Economic rules** (`autonomic-controller/src/economic_rules.rs`)
- `SurvivalRule`: balance / burn_rate → EconomicMode transitions with hysteresis
- `SpendVelocityRule`: cost_last_5_min > threshold → tighten gates
- `BudgetExhaustionRule`: cost_remaining < 20% → Conservative mode

**Step 1.3: Operational + cognitive rules**
- `ErrorStreakRule`: error_count/total > 30% → override to Verify mode
- `TokenExhaustionRule`: tokens_remaining < 10% → reduce max_tool_calls to 2
- `ContextPressureRule`: context_pressure > 0.8 → suggest model downgrade

**Step 1.4: Lago subscriber** (`autonomic-lago/src/subscriber.rs`)
- Subscribe to Lago journal via `Journal.stream(session_id, branch_id, 0)`
- Spawn per-session tailing tasks
- Feed events to controller engine, update shared projection state

**Step 1.5: HTTP API** (`autonomic-api/src/router.rs`)
- `GET /gating/{session_id}` → evaluate rules against projection → return `AutonomicGatingProfile`
- `GET /projection/{session_id}` → return raw `HomeostaticState` for debugging
- `GET /health` → daemon health check

**Step 1.6: Arcan integration** (minimal, ~20 lines)
- In `aios-runtime/src/lib.rs`, add optional `autonomic_url` field to `KernelRuntimeConfig`
- Before `estimate_mode()`, call `GET /gating/{session_id}` with 500ms timeout
- On success: use Autonomic's mode override + economic gates
- On failure: fall back to existing `estimate_mode()` (Autonomic is advisory, not blocking)

**Files to modify outside Autonomic:**
- `aiOS/crates/aios-runtime/src/lib.rs` — add ~20 lines for optional Autonomic HTTP call
- No Arcan changes needed (Arcan uses aios-runtime)

**Tests:**
- Projection: apply sequence of events, verify accumulated state matches expected
- Rules: each rule fires at correct thresholds with hysteresis
- Integration: spawn Lago journal (tempdir) + autonomicd, inject events, verify GET /gating response
- Determinism: same event sequence → identical GatingProfile

---

### Phase 2 — Economic Identity + Ledger (weeks 3–4)

**Goal**: Agents have secp256k1 addresses; balance is replayable from event stream.

- `identity.rs`: keypair generation via `k256`, address derivation via SHA-256, encrypted key storage as Lago blob
- Auto-create identity on first CostCharged event for a new agent
- `IdentityCreated` event → Lago journal via `autonomic-lago/publisher.rs`
- Replay test: destroy + restart autonomicd, verify ProjectionMap matches pre-restart state

---

### Phase 3 — x402 Payments (weeks 5–6)

**Goal**: Inter-agent micro-credit transfers via HTTP 402 protocol.

- `payment.rs`: `PaymentRequired` / `PaymentToken` types, secp256k1 signature
- `POST /payment/sign` — Autonomic signs payment requests
- Arcan `x402_request` tool: intercepts 402, calls Autonomic, retries
- `CreditTransfer` events written to Lago

---

### Phase 4 — Operational + Cognitive Homeostasis Maturity (weeks 7–8)

**Goal**: Full three-pillar regulation replaces hardcoded aios-runtime heuristics.

- Complete operational_rules (AgentStateVector → OperatingMode override)
- Complete cognitive_rules (token_usage + context_pressure → model tier + max_tokens)
- Arcan fully consults AutonomicGatingProfile for model selection + tool gating
- Optional: promote stable economic events from Custom to canonical EventKind

---

### Phase 5 — Survival Pressure + Replication (weeks 9–10)

**Goal**: Agents respond to economic pressure; viable agents can fork.

- `Hibernate` → Arcan skips LLM calls, heartbeats only
- `Hustle` → cheapest model, no expensive tools
- Lago branching exposed via arcan-lago bridge
- `POST /replicate/{session_id}` → child branch, seed credits, `AgentSpawned` event

---

## Communication Pattern

```
Arcan (tick_on_branch)
  │
  ├─ 1. Appends events to Lago journal (existing behavior, unchanged)
  │
  ├─ 2. GET /gating/{session_id} ──→ autonomicd (500ms timeout)
  │        AutonomicGatingProfile ←──────────────┘
  │
  └─ 3. Gates next action on profile (mode, model, tool permissions)

autonomicd
  │
  ├─ Subscribes to Lago SSE/journal stream (via autonomic-lago)
  ├─ Maintains per-session HomeostaticState projections
  ├─ Evaluates rule engine with hysteresis
  └─ Writes AutonomicDecision events back to Lago
```

**Pull model**: Arcan consults Autonomic before each tick. No Arcan architectural change. Failures are non-fatal (advisory).

---

## Reusable Existing Code

| What | Where | How Autonomic Uses It |
|------|-------|----------------------|
| `GatingProfile` struct | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:37-63` | Embedded in `AutonomicGatingProfile.operational` |
| `OperatingMode` enum | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:15-29` | Used in `HomeostaticState.operational` |
| `AgentStateVector` + `BudgetState` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-445` | Consumed from `StateEstimated` events |
| `EventKind` (all 55+ variants) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs:206+` | Projection reducer pattern-matches on these |
| `EventKind::Custom` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | Economic events wrapped as Custom |
| `EventStorePort` trait | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/ports.rs:136-153` | Autonomic reads/writes events via this |
| `Journal.stream()` → `EventTailStream` | `lago/crates/lago-journal/src/stream.rs` | Autonomic subscribes for real-time events |
| `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter` | `lago/crates/lago-aios-eventstore-adapter/src/lib.rs` | Bridges Lago storage to canonical EventStorePort |
| Arcan workspace Cargo.toml pattern | `arcan/Cargo.toml` (path deps, workspace config) | Template for autonomic/Cargo.toml |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Phase 0: workspace compiles
cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace

# Phase 1: cost accounting + gating
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- economic_rules
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- projection
cargo test -p autonomic-api -- routes

# End-to-end (Phase 1):
# 1. Start lago (tempdir journal)
# 2. Start autonomicd --lago-url http://localhost:3001
# 3. Inject CostCharged events via lago-cli or gRPC
# 4. GET /gating/{session_id} → assert correct EconomicMode

# Cross-project validation after each phase:
(cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd aiOS && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd arcan && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd lago && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace)
```

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Path dep resolution across 4 sibling repos | Follow exact Arcan Cargo.toml pattern: `path = "../aiOS/crates/..."` |
| Rust 2024 edition gotchas (gen keyword, unsafe set_var) | Same MSRV 1.85 / edition 2024 as all other projects |
| Autonomic latency blocking Arcan loop | 500ms HTTP timeout; fallback to default GatingProfile on failure |
| Projection staleness | Track `last_event_seq` in projection; return staleness indicator in API |
| Economic mode flapping | HysteresisGate with enter/exit thresholds + min-hold duration |
| Scope creep beyond Phase 0 | Phase 0 is types + stubs only; real logic starts Phase 1 |

---

## Immediate Next Step (Phase 0 Implementation)

Create the 5-crate workspace scaffold with all type definitions, stub implementations, and Phase 0 tests. Target: `cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace` green.

toolUseResult.content:
# Autonomic: Homeostasis Controller for the Agent OS

## Context

The aiOS/Arcan/Lago stack has cognitive continuity (Arcan) and event persistence (Lago) but lacks a **regulation layer**. The CLAUDE.md already names it: *Autonomic — homeostasis controller and simulation kernel for agent stability regulation*. Today, homeostasis is hardcoded in `aios-runtime::apply_homeostasis_controllers()` with magic numbers and no economic awareness. Autonomic externalizes this into a proper rule engine with three co-equal pillars: operational, cognitive, and economic homeostasis.

The key insight from the Conway Research context is that **economics is a core concern from crate zero** — not a bolt-on. Agents need survival pressure: budget accountability, cost-aware model selection, and identity-based payments.

---

## Current State Assessment

### What Already Exists

| Component | Location | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| `GatingProfile` (operational) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:37-63` | Defined, NOT consumed by runtime |
| `OperatingMode` (6 variants) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:15-29` | Defined and used |
| `AgentStateVector` + `BudgetState` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-445` | Defined, budget not actively decremented |
| Homeostasis events (Heartbeat, StateEstimated, BudgetUpdated, ModeChanged, GatesUpdated, CircuitBreakerTripped) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | In EventKind taxonomy |
| `EventKind::Custom { event_type, data }` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | Forward-compatible, round-trips through Lago |
| `EventStorePort` trait | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/ports.rs:136-153` | Canonical, implemented by Lago adapter |
| Lago `Journal.stream()` → `EventTailStream` | `lago/crates/lago-journal/src/stream.rs` | Tailing subscription via broadcast channel |
| `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter` | `lago/crates/lago-aios-eventstore-adapter/src/lib.rs` | Bridges Lago → canonical EventStorePort |
| Arcan middleware hooks (4 points) | `arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/runtime.rs` lines 304, 331, 391, 458 | `before_model`, `after_model`, `pre_tool`, `post_tool` |
| `TokenUsage` tracking | `arcan-core` `ModelTurn.usage` → `RunOutput.total_usage` | Tokens tracked, cost NOT computed |
| Hardcoded homeostasis | `aiOS/crates/aios-runtime/src/lib.rs:891-923` | Magic numbers, no hysteresis, no economics |

### Key Gap: GatingProfile Divergence

The existing `GatingProfile` (aios-protocol) has 6 **operational** fields. Autonomic needs **economic** fields: `economic_mode`, `max_tokens_next_turn`, `preferred_model`, `allow_expensive_tools`, `allow_replication`.

**Decision**: Autonomic defines `AutonomicGatingProfile` which embeds the canonical `GatingProfile` and adds economic extensions. This respects the kernel contract boundary. Arcan can consume the canonical portion immediately and adopt economic fields incrementally.

### Key Gap: Economic Events

No economic event types exist in `EventKind`.

**Decision**: Use `EventKind::Custom` with `event_type` prefixed `"autonomic."` (e.g., `"autonomic.CostCharged"`, `"autonomic.EconomicModeChanged"`). This is forward-compatible — the `custom_event_survives_round_trip` test in lago-aios-eventstore-adapter confirms Custom events round-trip through Lago. Promote to canonical EventKind variants once stabilized.

---

## Workspace Layout

Location: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/live/autonomic/` (can become a git submodule later, following arcan/lago pattern).

```
autonomic/
  Cargo.toml                        # workspace root
  CLAUDE.md                         # project conventions
  crates/
    autonomic-core/                 # Types, traits, errors
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: aios-protocol, thiserror, serde, chrono, k256, sha2
      src/
        lib.rs                      # module declarations + re-exports
        error.rs                    # AutonomicError (thiserror)
        economic.rs                 # EconomicMode, EconomicState, CostEstimator, SurvivalRule
        identity.rs                 # EconomicIdentity (secp256k1 keypair, address)
        gating.rs                   # AutonomicGatingProfile, EconomicGates, HomeostaticState
        events.rs                   # AutonomicEvent constructors (build Custom EventKind)
        rules.rs                    # HomeostaticRule trait, RuleSet
        hysteresis.rs               # HysteresisGate primitive

    autonomic-controller/           # Pure rule engine — no I/O
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core
      src/
        lib.rs
        engine.rs                   # ControllerEngine: rules × state → GatingProfile
        projection.rs               # Apply events → HomeostaticState (pure fold)
        economic_rules.rs           # SurvivalRule: balance × burn_rate → EconomicMode
        cognitive_rules.rs          # ContextPressureRule: token usage → model downgrade
        operational_rules.rs        # ModeRule: state_vector → OperatingMode override

    autonomic-lago/                 # Lago bridge — event subscription + publishing
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core, autonomic-controller, lago-core, lago-journal, tokio
      src/
        lib.rs
        subscriber.rs               # Lago Journal.stream() → feed events to controller
        publisher.rs                # Write AutonomicDecision events back to Lago

    autonomic-api/                  # axum HTTP server
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: autonomic-core, autonomic-controller, axum, tokio, serde_json
      src/
        lib.rs
        router.rs                   # GET /gating/{session_id}, GET /health, GET /projection/{session_id}
        state.rs                    # Shared AppState: Arc<RwLock<ProjectionMap>>

    autonomicd/                     # Binary daemon
      Cargo.toml                    # deps: all four crates + anyhow, tracing, clap
      src/
        main.rs                     # tokio::main, config, signal handling
        config.rs                   # AutonomicConfig (Lago URL, bind addr, setpoints)
```

**Crate dependency order:**
```
aios-protocol (from ../aiOS/)
    │
    v
autonomic-core (types + traits, zero Arcan/Lago deps)
    │          \
    v           v
autonomic-controller    autonomic-lago (+ lago-core, lago-journal)
    │          /
    v         v
autonomic-api (axum)
    │
    v
autonomicd (binary)
```

---

## Phased Build Plan

### Phase 0 — Workspace Scaffold (this session)

**Goal**: All 5 crates compile. `cargo check --workspace` and `cargo test --workspace` pass (no tests yet besides stubs).

**Files to create:**

   - `gating.rs` — `AutonomicGatingProfile` (embeds canonical `GatingProfile` + `EconomicGates`), `HomeostaticState` (three-pillar: operational/cognitive/economic)

  └─ Writes AutonomicDecision events back to Lago
```

**Pull model**: Arcan consults Autonomic before each tick. No Arcan architectural change. Failures are non-fatal (advisory).

---

## Reusable Existing Code

| What | Where | How Autonomic Uses It |
|------|-------|----------------------|
| `GatingProfile` struct | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:37-63` | Embedded in `AutonomicGatingProfile.operational` |
| `OperatingMode` enum | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/mode.rs:15-29` | Used in `HomeostaticState.operational` |
| `AgentStateVector` + `BudgetState` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/state.rs:398-445` | Consumed from `StateEstimated` events |
| `EventKind` (all 55+ variants) | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs:206+` | Projection reducer pattern-matches on these |
| `EventKind::Custom` | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/event.rs` | Economic events wrapped as Custom |
| `EventStorePort` trait | `aiOS/crates/aios-protocol/src/ports.rs:136-153` | Autonomic reads/writes events via this |
| `Journal.stream()` → `EventTailStream` | `lago/crates/lago-journal/src/stream.rs` | Autonomic subscribes for real-time events |
| `lago-aios-eventstore-adapter` | `lago/crates/lago-aios-eventstore-adapter/src/lib.rs` | Bridges Lago storage to canonical EventStorePort |
| Arcan workspace Cargo.toml pattern | `arcan/Cargo.toml` (path deps, workspace config) | Template for autonomic/Cargo.toml |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Phase 0: workspace compiles
cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace

# Phase 1: cost accounting + gating
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- economic_rules
cargo test -p autonomic-controller -- projection
cargo test -p autonomic-api -- routes

# End-to-end (Phase 1):
# 1. Start lago (tempdir journal)
# 2. Start autonomicd --lago-url http://localhost:3001
# 3. Inject CostCharged events via lago-cli or gRPC
# 4. GET /gating/{session_id} → assert correct EconomicMode

# Cross-project validation after each phase:
(cd autonomic && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd aiOS && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd arcan && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace) && \
(cd lago && cargo fmt && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace)
```

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Path dep resolution across 4 sibling repos | Follow exact Arcan Cargo.toml pattern: `path = "../aiOS/crates/..."` |
| Rust 2024 edition gotchas (gen keyword, unsafe set_var) | Same MSRV 1.85 / edition 2024 as all other projects |
| Autonomic latency blocking Arcan loop | 500ms HTTP timeout; fallback to default GatingProfile on failure |
| Projection staleness | Track `last_event_seq` in projection; return staleness indicator in API |
| Economic mode flapping | HysteresisGate with enter/exit thresholds + min-hold duration |
| Scope creep beyond Phase 0 | Phase 0 is types + stubs only; real logic starts Phase 1 |

---

## Immediate Next Step (Phase 0 Implementation)

Create the 5-crate workspace scaffold with all type definitions, stub implementations, and Phase 0 tests. Target: `cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace` green.
